title: Class Assignment: Exile Experiences
text:
Exile Experiences




βÄ€In the third year of the reign of Jehoiakim king of Judah, Nebuchadnezzar the king of Babylon came to Jerusalem and besieged it.βÄù (Daniel 1:1βÄ™2, LEB)[1]





My name is Geula [DGW1]¬†(Ή£÷Α÷ΦΉêΉï÷ΦΉ€÷ΗΉî), and I was only about ten when they took many of my friends to Babylon. As a girl, I wasnβÄôt allowed to enjoy the learning of the boys, but I knew Daniel and the three shovavim that would end up following him. Daniels younger brothers were my friends. They even taught me to read Hebrew by scratching the letters into the dirt. They agreed it was a silly rule and we all thought weβÄôd change it when I grew up and made the rules. ThatβÄôs when Babylon came, defeated our soldiers, and our king switched allegiances. Babylon took many of my friends. My brother Aharon was killed that day, with so many others, before the king switched sides. The boys were taken during the first deportation, while I remained in Jerusalem under the vassal king, Jehoiakim. 


Loosing many of my friends, especially the βÄ‰smartβÄô ones was hard. I still practiced my letters when nobody was looking, brushing it away quickly if any adults came looking. After the breaking, one day, I looked down the road and AliyahβÄôs parents were walking toward our bakery. I expected to see Aliyah, but she wouldnβÄôt be there, then I remembered she was taken, and my chest ached. Her parentsβÄô shoulders were slumped forward, their faces seemed hollow and sunken. I offered them a bite of our freshest bread. I think they saw her in me, because AliyahβÄôs ima would jostle my hair and offer a tight smile, then wipe her eyes a little before she would turn away to speak with my parents. When she did that, my eyes burned, my chest felt tight, like I couldnβÄôt breath, but I shoved it down, to be strong for AliyahβÄôs ima. Most days, though, I just felt numb, like my laughter had disappeared. I helped around the bakery, eventually taking it over as I got older.


The adults whispered about how the Assyrians took the Northern Kingdom of Israel away many generations ago, but they never believed it could happen to us, not in Yehuda, especially not in Yerushalaym, the very home of HaShem. Then again, my parents argued bitterly with The Cohanim, that if they had not allowed all of the other Elohim such prominence in our high places, HaShem would not have allowed Babylon to come in the first place. The Cohanim waved them off dismissively. 


In many ways, life seemed to carry on normally, until I was 18. I was running the bakery with my ima when some the shofar began blowing all throughout the city, and men came running down the streets yelling that the Babylonians had come, with Chaldeans, Aram, Moab, and Ammonites as well. People began throwing closed their shops, locking their doors, and looking for the hiding places weβÄôd dug out in case this day ever came. I couldnβÄôt move, I was too numb, too frozen. I watched the chaos begin around me, while my ima and I just stood there, covered in flour dust. Eventually, aba came into the room and found us just standing there, and he shook us into movement. We closed everything, and went into hiding. We stayed there for months, eating what bread we had stored, making little cakes over dung fires from our storage reserves. We heard about horrible things people were doing in other parts of the city just to eat something. 


The Vassal King, Jehoiakim thought he would ignore the Tributes we owed to Babylon after he initially switched allegiances from Egypt to Babylon. Nebuchadnezzar didnβÄôt like that much and showed up outside our walls. ItβÄôs been almost a year now, and we have no food left. We suck on bread sacks, hoping for a bit of the stale flour. My Parents have discussed sneaking passed the city guards to surrender to the Babylonians, because that couldnβÄôt be worse then living in the city, could it? Then, we got word this evening that Jehoiakim died mysteriously, nobody was very upset about it or asked too many questions. The guards opened the gates and let the invaders come in, surrendering to them, placing the body of Jehoiakim in front of the gate. What will this mean for me? What will happen to us? Could it be worse than starving to death in my own city, the city of HaShem, Yerushalaym? I will lay myself on HaShemβÄôs mercy, perhaps I can find favor in my new home, when they take me away, as long as stay faithful to Adonai, like my friends Daniel is rumored to have done.


¬†




βÄ€And these are the words of the letter that Jeremiah the prophet sent from Jerusalem to the remainder of the exiles, and to the priests, and to the prophets, and to all the people whom Nebuchadnezzar had deported from Jerusalem to BabylonβÄΠ βÄ€Thus says Yahweh of hosts, the God of Israel, to all the exiles whom I have deported from Jerusalem to Babylon, βÄ‰Build houses and live in them, and plant gardens and eat their fruit. Take wives and father sons and daughters, and take for your sons wives, and give your daughters to men that they may bear sons and daughters, and multiply there, and you must not be few. And seek the prosperity of the city where I have deported you, and pray on behalf of it to Yahweh, for in its prosperity you will have prosperity.βÄô For thus says Yahweh of hosts, the God of Israel, βÄ‰Do not let your prophets who are in your midst, and your diviners, deceive you, and you must not listen to your dreams that you are causing them to dream. For they are prophesying falsely to you in my name; I have not sent them,βÄô declares Yahweh. For thus says Yahweh, βÄ‰As soon as the time has passed, seventy years for Babylon, I will attend to you, and I will fulfill my good word to you, to bring you back to this place. For I know the plans that I am planning concerning you,βÄô declares Yahweh, βÄ‰plans for prosperity and not for harm, to give to you a future and a hope. Then when you call me, and you come and pray to me, then I will listen to you. When you search for me, then you will find me, if you seek me with all your heart. And I will let myself be found by you,βÄô declares Yahweh, βÄ‰and I will restore your fortunes, and I will gather you from all the nations and from all the places to which I have driven you,βÄô declares Yahweh, βÄ‰and I will bring you back to the place from which I deported you.βÄôβÄù (Jeremiah 29:1βÄ™14, LEB)





¬†








[1] The Lexham English Bible (LEB), Fourth Edition, Logo Bible Software, Harris, W. H., III, Ritzema, E., Brannan, R., Mangum, D., Dunham, J., Reimer, J. A., & Wierenga, M. (Eds.) (Bellingham, WA: Lexham Press, 2010), Referenced during this reflection: Daniel 1:1βÄ™2, 2 Kings 23:34βÄ™24:7, 2 Chronicles 36:4βÄ™8, Jeremiah 22:18βÄ™19, 29:4βÄ™7, 36:23, http://www.lexhampress.com.














¬†[DGW1]A Hebrew name that represents βÄ€returnβÄù or βÄ€end of an eraβÄù is βÄ€GeulaβÄù (Ή£÷Α÷ΦΉêΉï÷ΦΉ€÷ΗΉî).


Geula (Ή£÷Α÷ΦΉêΉï÷ΦΉ€÷ΗΉî)



¬Ζ¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬† Meaning: The name Geula means βÄ€redemptionβÄù or βÄ€deliveranceβÄù in Hebrew.



¬Ζ¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬† Significance: In Jewish tradition, Geula signifies the act of being redeemed or liberated from a state of exile or hardship. It often refers to the prophesied future redemption of the Jewish people and the end of a difficult period, symbolizing both a return to a promised state and the end of an era of suffering.



¬Ζ¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬† Usage: Geula is used as a feminine given name in Hebrew-speaking communities.


Contextual Significance



¬Ζ¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬† Biblical References: The concept of Geula is rooted in the Hebrew Bible, where it denotes GodβÄôs saving actions on behalf of His people.



¬Ζ¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬† Cultural Meaning: The name embodies hope, renewal, and the transition into a new and better phase, making it synonymous with the end of an era and the beginning of another.


Alternative Names



¬Ζ¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬† Shuv (Ή©Ή¹Ήï÷ΦΉë): Derived from the Hebrew root meaning βÄ€return,βÄù though itβÄôs less commonly used as a given name.



¬Ζ¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬† Nehemiah (Ή†÷ΑΉ½÷ΕΉû÷ΑΉô÷ΗΉî): Meaning βÄ€God comforts,βÄù associated with the return to Jerusalem and rebuilding after the Babylonian exile.



¬Ζ¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬† Aliyah (ΉΔ÷≤Ή€÷¥Ήô÷Η÷ΦΉî): Meaning βÄ€ascentβÄù or βÄ€going up,βÄù commonly used to refer to the act of immigrating to Israel, symbolizing a return to the homeland.

title: The Freedom Center βÄ™> This is just a churchβÄΠ isnβÄôt it? SighβÄΠ.
text:
IβÄôm the guy who keeps desperately trying to NOT be a βÄ€pastorβÄù has this idea. While specifics have changes, this core idea has been growing since at least 2003βÄΠ. βÄ€ItβÄôs not a church, itβÄôsβÄΠ.βÄù





I looked at this summary below of my ideas (but better organized by AI) and thought to myself,







βÄ€This is a church, isnβÄôt it?¬†


There is no concert followed by a public speaker (although I could see how that could happen too)βÄΠ¬†


This is just a different expression of, and reimagining of, a church in a new format. A βÄ€Fresh ExpressionβÄù. I would want to make sure the format and resources were always free, like the BibleProject has modeled.


But at the heart, this is just a church isnβÄôt it?¬†


After all that runningβÄΠ Am I just βÄ€pastorβÄù hiding behind other titles? Ha haβÄΠ SighβÄΠ.βÄù







WellβÄΠ IβÄôll just sit with that feeling for now. IβÄôm not sure what to do with it. I donβÄôt have to βÄ€makeβÄù anything happen. The idea has been percolating since at least 2003, it can keep percolating until the path becomes clear. ButβÄΠ IβÄôm really afraid this is a church. Ha ha haβÄΠ¬†









AI Response (DeepSeek) - 2:




The Freedom Center¬†is a fantastic nameβÄîit evokes liberation, growth, and exploration, which aligns perfectly with your vision. LetβÄôs refine and expand the concept with this name in mind, ensuring it captures the essence of what youβÄôre trying to build.



Mission Statement for The Freedom Center


βÄ€The Freedom Center is a community dedicated to fostering spiritual, intellectual, and emotional growth. We provide a safe space for individuals to explore ancient wisdom, develop critical thinking skills, and engage in meaningful dialogueβÄîfree from dogma, judgment, or pressure. Through interdisciplinary learning, therapeutic support, and communal reflection, we empower people to wrestle with big questions, grow in self-awareness, and apply timeless insights to modern life.βÄù



Core Values of The Freedom Center



Freedom: Liberation from rigid doctrines, creeds, and traditions; freedom to question, explore, and grow.


Curiosity: A commitment to asking questions, seeking understanding, and embracing uncertainty.


Community: A safe, inclusive space where people can share, learn, and grow together.


Wisdom: Engaging with ancient texts and ideas as sources of meditative wisdom, not prescriptive rules.


Integration: Blending spiritual, intellectual, and emotional growth into a holistic approach to life.




Programs and Offerings



Freedom Circles¬†(Small Groups):


Bible Exploration: Contextual, ANE/2nd Temple-focused readings of Scripture as wisdom literature.


Wisdom Dialogues: Discussions on philosophical, theological, and psychological topics.


Therapeutic Groups: Facilitated sessions focused on emotional and spiritual growth.




Freedom Labs¬†(Workshops and Courses):


Critical Thinking 101: Teaching skills for analyzing ideas, avoiding fallacies, and engaging in constructive dialogue.


Ancient Wisdom for Modern Life: Applying biblical and philosophical insights to contemporary challenges.


Emotional Intelligence: Workshops on self-awareness, empathy, and healthy communication.




Freedom Forums¬†(Events and Guest Speakers):


Scholar Series: Inviting biblical scholars, theologians, and philosophers to share their insights.


Community Conversations: Open forums on topics like justice, forgiveness, love, and meaning.


Creative Expression: Art, music, and writing workshops to explore spirituality and self-expression.




Freedom Therapy¬†(Therapeutic Support):


Lay Counseling: Trained facilitators offering non-clinical support for emotional and spiritual growth.


Therapist Partnerships: Collaborating with licensed professionals for clinical needs.


Support Groups: Focused on specific issues like grief, addiction, or relationships.




Freedom Online¬†(Digital Resources):


Resource Library: Articles, videos, and courses on biblical studies, critical thinking, and emotional growth.


Virtual Freedom Circles: Online small groups for remote participants.


Podcast/YouTube Channel: Sharing teachings, interviews, and discussions.






Structure and Operations



Local Chapters:


Meeting Spaces: Borrowed from community centers, churches, or homes.


Facilitators: Trained volunteers who guide discussions and ensure a safe environment.


Autonomy: Local groups operate independently but align with the core principles of The Freedom Center.




Governance:


Advisory Board: Composed of scholars, therapists, and community leaders to provide guidance.


Core Team: A small group overseeing operations, resources, and partnerships.




Funding Model:


Donations: Voluntary contributions from participants (like AA/Al-Anon).


Workshop Fees: Charging modest fees for courses or events to sustain operations.


Grants: Seeking funding from foundations that support education, mental health, or spiritual growth.






Branding and Identity



Logo and Visuals:


A logo that combines elements of openness (e.g., an open door, a tree, or a circle) with symbols of growth and exploration.


A color palette that feels warm, inviting, and modern (e.g., earthy tones with accents of blue or gold).




Tone and Voice:


Conversational: Approachable and inclusive, avoiding overly academic or religious jargon.


Empowering: Encouraging curiosity, growth, and self-discovery.


Hopeful: Emphasizing freedom, transformation, and community.






First Steps to Launch



Pilot Program:


Start with a small group (in-person or online) to test the format and gather feedback.


Focus on one or two core offerings (e.g., a Bible Exploration group and a Critical Thinking workshop).




Build a Team:


Recruit facilitators, therapists, and scholars who share your vision.


Train them in the principles and practices of The Freedom Center.




Create Resources:


Develop materials for Bible study, critical thinking, and emotional growth.


Record introductory videos or podcasts to explain the vision and invite participation.




Promote the Initiative:


Use social media, local networks, and partnerships to spread the word.


Host a launch event or open house to introduce The Freedom Center to the community.






Long-Term Vision


The Freedom Center could grow into a network of local and online communities, offering a unique blend of spiritual, intellectual, and emotional support. Over time, it could become a movement that helps people engage with ancient wisdom, develop critical thinking skills, and grow in emotional intelligenceβÄîall within a supportive, non-dogmatic community.


By staying true to your core values and fostering a culture of curiosity and compassion, The Freedom Center could become a transformative force in the lives of countless individuals.











AI Response (DeepSeek) - 1:



Your vision is both ambitious and deeply meaningful, blending spiritual exploration, intellectual growth, and emotional support into a cohesive community experience. To organize this into something coherent, letβÄôs break it down into core components, principles, and actionable steps, drawing inspiration from the structure of groups like Al-Anon or AA while tailoring it to your unique goals.



Core Principles and Practices



Shared Practices and Principles¬†(like AA/Al-Anon):


Non-dogmatic Approach: Avoid rigid doctrines, creeds, or βÄ€we believeβÄù statements. Focus on exploration, questioning, and personal growth.


Community-Centered: Emphasize shared learning and mutual support rather than hierarchical leadership.


Anonymity and Safety: Create a safe space where people can share openly without fear of judgment or proselytization.


Voluntary Participation: No pressure to conform or commit; people can engage as much or as little as they feel comfortable.




Biblical Engagement:


Contextual Reading: Teach participants to read the Bible through the lens of its Ancient Near Eastern (ANE) and Second Temple worldview.


Wisdom Meditation: Approach the text as meditative wisdom literature rather than prescriptive law.


Theological Wrestling: Encourage deep, honest engagement with the text and its implications for modern life.




Critical Thinking and Emotional Growth:


Critical Thinking Skills: Teach participants how to analyze ideas, ask questions, and engage in constructive dialogue.


Emotional Intelligence: Foster curiosity, empathy, and the ability to engage without defensiveness.


Therapeutic Support: Offer group sessions that blend spiritual and emotional growth, with referrals to licensed therapists when needed.




Holistic Integration:


Interdisciplinary Approach: Combine insights from academic biblical studies, philosophy, theology, and psychology.


Practical Application: Help participants apply ancient wisdom to contemporary challenges in a thoughtful, nuanced way.






Structure and Format



Local Groups:


Meeting Spaces: Borrow space from community centers, churches, libraries, or even homes (like AA/Al-Anon).


Regular Gatherings: Weekly or bi-weekly meetings with a flexible structure (e.g., discussion, meditation, teaching, or group therapy).


Facilitators: Trained lay leaders who guide discussions and ensure a safe, respectful environment.




Program Offerings:


Bible Study Groups: Focused on contextual reading and wisdom meditation.


Critical Thinking Workshops: Teach skills for analyzing ideas and engaging in dialogue.


Therapeutic Groups: Facilitated discussions on emotional and spiritual growth, with referrals to licensed therapists as needed.


Guest Speakers: Invite scholars, therapists, or theologians to share insights on specific topics.




Online Presence:


Resource Hub: Offer articles, videos, and courses on biblical studies, critical thinking, and emotional growth.


Virtual Meetings: Allow people to participate remotely, fostering a broader community.




Partnerships:


Therapists: Collaborate with licensed professionals to provide clinical support when needed.


Academic Institutions: Partner with seminaries or universities to offer accredited courses or certifications.


Existing Organizations: Work with groups like Fuller Theological Seminary or local churches to expand reach and resources.






Name and Identity


Your initiative needs a name that reflects its purpose and values. Consider something that evokes exploration, community, and growth. Examples:



The Wrestling Ground: Emphasizes the idea of wrestling with God and ideas.


Wisdom Commons: Highlights the communal pursuit of wisdom.


Open Table: Suggests inclusivity and shared exploration.


The Thinking Faith Collective: Combines intellectual and spiritual growth.




Governance and Funding



Governance:


Local Autonomy: Like AA/Al-Anon, allow local groups to operate independently while adhering to core principles.


Advisory Board: Form a board of scholars, therapists, and community leaders to provide guidance and oversight.




Funding:


Donations: Rely on voluntary contributions from participants (like AA/Al-Anon).


Grants: Seek funding from foundations that support education, mental health, or spiritual growth.


Workshops/Courses: Offer paid courses or certifications to sustain the initiative.






First Steps



Define Your Vision: Write a clear mission statement and core principles.


Start Small: Launch a pilot group in your local area or online.


Build a Team: Recruit facilitators, therapists, and scholars who share your vision.


Create Resources: Develop materials for Bible study, critical thinking, and emotional growth.


Promote the Initiative: Use social media, local networks, and partnerships to spread the word.




Long-Term Vision


Over time, your initiative could grow into a network of local and online communities, offering a unique blend of spiritual, intellectual, and emotional support. By staying true to your core principles and fostering a culture of curiosity and compassion, you could create a movement that helps people engage with ancient wisdom in a way that is both authentic and transformative.


This is a big idea, but itβÄôs also deeply needed in todayβÄôs world. By starting small and staying focused on your vision, you can build something truly impactful.













AI Prompt (DeepSeek):


Background: Al-Anon/AA/NA/CoDA/Celebrate Recovery all operate on a similar principle. The meeting location is largely irrelevant (wherever space can be found), but there is shared set of practices and principles upon which they internally operate, and most of them share a large overlap of those even with each other. They arenβÄôt a βÄ€ChurchβÄù, they donβÄôt own buildings, they donβÄôt even have LLCs in most cases, although they have some governance locally and nationally (albeit loosely).¬† ¬†βÄîβÄ™> In much the same way, IβÄôm feeling an increasing desire over the last decade to startβÄΠ. SOMETHING?βÄΠ. related to both helping people read the bible more authentically (free from doctrines, creeds, and traditions, and βÄ‰we believeβÄô commitments) while also providing a place for people to think better. Learn critical thinking skills, how to get curious and not defensive, etc. How to understand the biblical authors ANE/2nd Temple worldview, what it means to read the bible as wisdom meditation literature and not βÄ€lawβÄù, and then think about that wisdom in todayβÄôs context theologically. Not stripping words and blindly applying them to today, but really βÄ€wrestling with GodβÄù as Jacob is said to have done, and do so in community. I would also like to offer a variety of group sessions that border on therapy or at least therapeutic, and partner with licensed therapists for those who need clinical assistance, while offering (very well trained) βÄ€lay counselingβÄù, in line with Siang-Yang TanβÄôs philosophy. In some ways, Academic Biblical Studies, Critical Thinking courses, Philosophy, Theology, and Psychology seem a disparate group of things to homogenize into a single (?Group, Organization, Event, Label?). But in other ways, it seems natural, and indeed Fuller Theological Seminary blends many Theology/Psychology programs. Help me organize this thought into something coherent.

   



Subscribe Now - Pursue a No Hiding Lifestyle¬†









 Shalom: Live Long and Prosper!

title: Returning to The OnenessβÄΠ a spiritual cloud drive?
text:
My father was a pastor in the Disciples of Christ (DOC) denomination of US-American Churchianity. He had an MDiv and DMin, and pastored ~1978-1996. He got his MFT after becoming an atheist and left the church.




I saw the spiritual world a lot as a child, I saw Jesus, I saw a demon, and I sawβÄΠ ThingsβÄΠ¬†





I became a Fundamentalist, and then a PentecostalβÄΠ And then left Churchianity myself. But I never left the core conviction that Jesus was real. All the things I didnβÄôt understand and got wrong never really took that conviction away.¬†





I now know that Genesis 1-11 was written by the biblical authors and redactors as a polemic counter narrative to the Babylonian creation mythologies. I know it was written in exile, and it was NEVER intended to be read literally. The earth is billions of years old and multiple types of hominids roamed this planet and even interbred before Homo Sapiens became the last hominids. We created cultures starting about 200,000 years ago, maybe as long as 500,000βÄΠ Evidence evolving on our understanding of that ancient past.¬†





But the core conviction that Jesus died and rose again, and is, and I am returning to him when I die, and might be coming back never leaves me.¬†





Quantum physics creates a whole other path to pondering bigger ideas tooβÄΠ¬†





And then I think about other creation mythologies, like the various Indigenous Americans have, and I wonder βÄΠ.¬†





This got me thinking about Cloud Drives, Google Drive, One Drive, BoxβÄΠ¬†





When I upload files they donβÄôt become nebulous indistinct entities. They stay in their form, and I can reprint them and turn them back into physical documents.¬†





What if our consciousness is like that, and God is like a Hard Drive?





Just thinkingβÄΠ


Darrell Wolfe

title: Why Storytelling?
text:
Storytelling is the lifeblood of the human race. Sure, many have replaced books with movies or YouTube videos, but great stories transcend.From ancient times, The Storyteller, was a guest of kings and paupers. He/She was the keeper of legacies, histories, and entertainment. Unlike the best selling βÄ€5 secrets to a better βÄΠ.βÄù or βÄ€10 things you need to know aboutβÄΠβÄù, type books, stories stick with you. I canβÄôt remember the 7 habits, but I remember Frodo beating all odds to get that ring to the volcano.When you think about all the elements of your brand, story is among the most important parts. Once your brand story is simple and simply told, it informs the rest of your brand. It decides the fonts, colors, and styles you use.Your brand story will be the the thing you use to get who you are into the mouths of your truest fans, so they can tell others who you are. Often without knowing they are telling your story.Food for thoughtβÄΠHere are some clips from great articles about using story telling in Brand Marketing:FastCompany:¬†Why our brains crave storytelling in marketing

When reading straight data, only the language parts of our brains work to decode the meaning. But when we read a story, not only do the language parts of our brains light up, but any other part of the brain that we would use if we were actually experiencing what weβÄôre reading about becomes activated as well.

i-Scoop:¬†Questions to ask yourself about your brand story


What is the story and narrative behind everything you do as a brand, ranging from what you stand for to the reason why you developed solution X or decided to support βÄ‰good cause YβÄô?¬†




How can you get to that story thatβÄôs part of your brand and even peopleβÄôs DNA instead of to just the facts?¬†




How do you actually connect with people in the language they understand best: the language they can βÄ€visualizeβÄù in a story-like context?




What types of stories appeal to your content marketing βÄ€personasβÄù?




What about the stories your customers and βÄ‰audiencesβÄô are already telling? How do you listen to those and include them?¬†


Entrepreneur.Com:¬†Storytelling Could Bring Your Brand to Life and Strengthen Your Marketing Impact

A brandβÄôs story is no longer limited to or controlled by the media outlets and advertising that it uses. One would even say that this has been completely reversed: the media now runs stories based on how much traction theyβÄôve gained elsewhere. Your customers and advocates are now the tastemakers, giving power to the stories that resonate with them.

Forbes: 5 Secrets to Use Storytelling for Brand Marketing Success

Honesty and transparency are important in brand storytelling. Yes, youβÄôre crafting βÄ€stories,βÄù but they need to be rooted in the reality of your brand, products, and industry. In other words, even brand stories must adhere to the three primary steps of brand-building: consistency, persistence, and restraint.

Darrell

title: New Scene - White Noise
text:
The following is a re-write of what I posted recently. I like this MUCH better. 

***




Normally, the workshop would have his full attention. But his over-dose concoction left him more groggy than it had in days past.  After a shower, Ash opted for the front porch. He brought his coffee, thanks to the Kurieg, a Christmas gift from Sheriff Danny, and a microwaved breakfast sandwich. The wrap-around porch was situated with a view of the forest. Thanks to his familyβÄôs inheritance, he owned 100βÄôs of acres in any direction of the house, with the exception of an easement that served as an access road for his neighbors.  The sun was still at the back of the house, which left him in relative shade. Three white-tail deer were eating berries off of the wild bushes heβÄôd left to attract them. It was already hunting season, but they seemed to know they had sanctuary here. HeβÄôd never fired a weapon this close to the house and they seemed to know it.  The three deer each twitched their ears backward and raised their heads in unison. They looked back down the five-mile driveway. He called it a driveway, even though it was just a dirt road with rocks and a periodic plaining from Ben, the local snow plower, tow truck driver, plainer, and anything else that needed an occasional driver.  The subtle crunch of rock under boot caught AshβÄôs attention too. A shadowy figure was making their way down the driveway, through the trees. If they were there to hurt him, theyβÄôd be making a quieter approach; however, old training required he be cautious just in case.  The figure was still about a five minute walk away, Ash walked inside and grabbed his pistol, shoving into the holster under his flannel shirt; then took the rifle outside and laid it again the rail in front of the rocking chair before resuming his coffee and breakfast. Eventually, she came into view. A female, darker skin, crazy hair popping out the back of a bright multi-colored crocheted hat. The sun revealed a form-fitting joggers outfit, equally bright, mostly purple.  βÄ€IβÄôm sorry to bother you,βÄù She said with a warm voice as she approached. βÄ€βÄΠ but my car broke down a few miles down the road.βÄù She smiled with a the easy going smile of an ignorant city girl.  βÄ€You should have called a cab, before walking through the woods alone,βÄù Ash gestured to her, βÄ€in that.βÄù  βÄ€WhatβÄôs that supposed to mean, mister? βÄ‰In thatβÄô? WhatβÄôs wrong with this?βÄù He was about to answer, but she waived it off. βÄ€Besides, I tried to call a tow truck, but thereβÄôs no cell signal out here. ItβÄôs like I stepped back in time to the stone ages.βÄù Her skin flushed a shade red, and bit her lip.  A lump caught his throat, as he realized heβÄôd upset her. Ash wasnβÄôt used to prolonged human interaction with strangers. He didnβÄôt even sell his own goods but used the local consignment shop to sell them so he could avoid these unpleasant conversations. βÄ€IβÄôm sorry. I didnβÄôt mean to offend. ItβÄôs just that those bright colors could attract the wrong kind of attention from animals that could see you as a threat.βÄù  She looked down at the ground, and kicked a small rock aside. βÄ€IβÄôm sorry too. IβÄôm working on my temper.βÄù She looked up again, and caught his eye, and hesitated. βÄ€I meanβÄΠ LetβÄôs start over. Can I borrow your phone?βÄù  βÄ€I would let you, except for I donβÄôt have one.βÄù  βÄ€You donβÄôt have a phone? No cell, no land line, nothing?βÄù The skin on her nose scrunched up, and her head tilted to one side. βÄ€How do you survive out here without a phone?βÄù  βÄ€I live here to avoid civilization, I generally donβÄôt want to be reached.βÄù  βÄ€But what about emergencies?βÄù  βÄ€You mean like when lost young ladies break down on the road and come wandering onto my property? That wasnβÄôt in my original planning when I decided not to have a phone. Besides, I have a voice mail that I access only when I go into town and use the Library computer.βÄù  βÄ€Well, I guess weβÄôre getting a bit off track here. Can you help me find someone who does have a phone so I can call a Tow Truck?βÄù  βÄ€Sorry, no-one for miles. I keep it that way. Let me see if I can help.βÄù He motioned for her to follow him. She stood to the side as he grabbed his tool box and threw it in the back of the truck. He laid his rifle on the back seat and closed the door. He waived her into the passenger side as he climbed in the driverβÄôs seat.  βÄ€IβÄôm not sure I should be climbing into an ancient pick-up truck with a stranger.βÄù She winked and smiled, mischievously.  βÄ€Well, you can come inside a strangers cabin and wait for no-one to show up for weeks, or you can hop in the truck.βÄù He smiled back.  βÄ€Heard!βÄù She hopped in, put on her seat belt, and crossed her legs. βÄ€My nameβÄôs Destiny, yours?βÄù  βÄ€Ash.βÄù He replied.  βÄ€Ash, as in the ash left over after a camp fire?βÄù  βÄ€Something like that.βÄù  The engine turned over and rumbled to life. Her white teeth, and relaxed posture put him at ease in a way he hadnβÄôt been in a very long time. She chattered on about where sheβÄôd been, how she got onto the back roads, and where sheβÄôd come from. A reporter, following a lead about a reduction in small towns in America. Only bolstered by the fact she couldnβÄôt even find this one. The GPS had told her to take a side road to cut-off miles of the drive down a curvy highway, only to then have her lose the GPS signal before she found the highway again.  After about 30 minutes working on the electric SUV sheβÄôd rented, Ash realized he was in far over his head. Carburetors he understood, not this new computerized junk. βÄ€Well, I give up. You need a real mechanic.βÄù  βÄ€Well, thanks for trying.βÄù She looked around, possibly a little concerned after his warning about wild animals.  βÄ€DonβÄôt worry, IβÄôm not leaving a helpless city girl alone in the woods. IβÄôll drive you into town and get Ed to come pick it up and bring it to his shop. ThereβÄôs a small Motel in town you can stay at while he looks it over.βÄù   βÄ€Motel?βÄù DestinyβÄôs head shot back, eyebrows raised. βÄ€How long is this going to take?βÄù  βÄ€Things are pretty dead this time of year, so he can probably look at it right away. But weβÄôre not exactly an electric car mecca, so heβÄôll probably have to order parts or have the rental company send a new vehicle and pick this one up. Knowing this area, youβÄôre here until at least tomorrow.  βÄ€Well, IβÄôm not sure this is the town I was headed for, but it sounds like the perfect town for my story. Maybe IβÄôll just stay around a few days after all, make the trip worth it.βÄù  βÄ€Suit yourself,βÄù Ash said as he pulled into town from a side road. He pointed left, and her eyes followed, then pointed right. ThatβÄôs it. Welcome to Deer Ridge, Idaho. Population 420, no jokes please, weβÄôve heard βÄôem all.βÄù  She stifled a giggle. βÄ€OK. Fair enough.βÄù  βÄ€EdβÄôs Auto is up that way, to the left. CarrieβÄôs Ice Cream is open until Oktoberfest, so youβÄôll have that until then. She also serves some decent coffee. ThereβÄôs a small grocery there. And the rest of the shops are closed until next spring. You can walk the bay of the lake from just over that wall there. ItβÄôs pretty any time of year.βÄù  Ash pulled onto the only road, and stopped in front of EdβÄôs. After a few moments, Ed and Destiny were on their way to her car. She smiled again, and waved her fingers as they passed. His chest tightened, ears tingled, it took an extra moment to remember to breath. He hadnβÄôt felt like this in many decades. Shrugging it off, Ash grabbed a few things from the grocery before heading back to his refuge. 

***

   

Darrell G. Wolfe


 Storyteller | Writer | Thinker | Consultant |¬†Multipotentialite

title: The Shroud
text:
Shadow enveloped Stephen as he watched the movements below from his perch in the old church bell tower. His cloak danced in the breeze as a proud flag, his face enshrouded under the familiar hood. He watched the events of Ashland, Idaho with a passive interest, until he noticed someone looking at himβÄΠ directly?  It had been three hundred years since anyone had seen him, really seen him. Could this Captain Ashland be one of them? A seer? Unlikely. Not many were left in the world. Although the rumors of the shadow world claimed they were on the rise, ever since the Azusa Street Revival.  Stephen moved from his perch, pacing left to right. The CaptainβÄôs eyes followed him. βÄ€I must tell the others,βÄù he said. βÄ€There is an awakeningβÄΠβÄù  His form morphed into a black whisp of cloud and he¬†floated away from the perch, into the mountains.   ***  Ash noticed the figure evaporated into a cloud of smoke. He shook his head. When would these visions ever get better?   

Darrell G. Wolfe


 Storyteller | Writer | Thinker | Consultant |¬†Multipotentialite

title: Writing Craft: Getting UnStuck
text:
Getting UnStuck - Story Structure Guiding The Path

It quite common, especially for Pantsers (those who write by the seat of their pants with no real outline or plan ahead of time), to get stuck in your story. At some point, we hit a roadblock, writerβÄôs block, and canβÄôt think of what the story should be next.  Story Structure Clues  This is, in part, because we donβÄôt start with the outline that the stricture Outliners do. We canβÄôt even comprehend where the story will be going in order to outline.  However, if we have an idea of the path (the skeleton of the¬†structure), we can start to see a way through the hazy fog of the future. But even then, we can get stuck like I did last night.  IβÄôd written my¬†way through and just couldnβÄôt imagine what needed to happen next. I know where the next major plot point is going to be and what (generally) will happen (Destiny is caught by the FBI but Ash escapes). But itβÄôs way to early to write that in the story timeline. If I write it now, itβÄôll be out of left field. Nothing has progressed and itβÄôs just too early.  Scene Structure Clues  Then, I had to go back and look at my scenes. Every scene is a natural reaction to the one before it. The reaction becomes the action the next reacts to.  Even the very first/opening scene of the book is simply a reaction to events that took place before the reader arrived.  So I had to go back and re-read the scene that preceded to see what the natural reaction would be.  Pro Note: Bill Myers said that his routine is to start by editing whatever he wrote at the last writing session, then start the new writing. This allows you to become aware of the action and set up the reaction.  Up until this point, every reaction flowed effortlessly. I just saw the movie in my head and wrote it. This time, I just didnβÄôt see anything. Then two things happened.  #1. Questions: I asked questions about the previous scene and made notes of things to address.¬†  Small example, but, if Ash said he was hungry going to bed in the next scene doesnβÄôt make sense unless we address that. Did he go to bed hungry, did he eat? Maybe that drives the action to a kitchen or even out of the house to a restaurant?  My questions/items to address from the previous scene: 


What did Chester read thatΟΔβ²§β³Δs not good?


How do they react to a moment of closeness?


Ash is Hungry


HeΟΔβ²§β³Δs in the file so he wants to see it himself.¬†


He finds Eta in there, side note.


What else? What in there moves them forward?


 So IβÄôm starting the next scene with ONE simple reaction to the previous scene. One sentence. That will drive the next and the next until the movie reel starts to play.

ΟΔβ²§≈™WhatΟΔβ²§β³Δs not good?ΟΔβ²§¬ù Destiny sat a little straighter and leaned forward.


#2. The Other Guy: I have two characterβÄôs to keep track of. WhatβÄôs happening in the other guys world right now?

 This also reminded me that I have to Point of Views (POVs) running. The Blue and Light Blue scenes are both Ash (awake vs dreaming). I did light blue for his dreams so I could read just those and make sure thereβÄôs a progressive revelation in the dreams.  The Red scenes are those that follow along inside the head of Dr.¬†Briar. If I go back and read the last red scene, I can then βÄ€reactβÄù to that scene. This turned out to be a gold mine, because it set up some information that will eventually lead to DestinyβÄôs capture. I didnβÄôt know how it would happen before last night, and I now have a clue.  ReOrder  I did re-order the scenes this morning. I wrote Dr.¬†BriarβÄôs reaction scene, but it takes place the next morning. AshβÄôs reaction scene is still taking place last night. So I just need to swap the order and put AshβÄôs scene on top of Dr.¬†BriarβÄôs so that I donβÄôt get my days/times out of order and confuse the reader.  Go Back  Now I can go back and start with my opening sentence (DetinyβÄôs reaction) and see where AshβÄôs scene goes to finish out his turbulent day. 

Darrell G. Wolfe


Storyteller | Writer | Thinker | Consultant |¬†Multipotentialite

  The. EndβÄΠβÄΠ.. Not.

title: Writing Prompt | Room and Bored
text:
At the Heart Writers group meeting tonight, led by Lauren Stinton, we did a short writing prompt.  You have seven minutes to write (fiction or nonfiction) and it must include these three items/themes: 


Jesus is Lord of all


Princess


Cat


 Here is my result, with a bit of post-meeting editing and addition. Keep in mind, I wrote it in OneNote on my phoneβÄΠ 

***


Room and Bored

The light amplified through the double glass window pane, despite the clawed dusty drapes, warming PrincessβÄô furry belly. She was Queen of this castle and she held the honor with dignity.  Her human was a good servant. He brought her food at the appointed time. He changed her box. He performed his part admirably.  Then her life shattered.  Her human walked in the front door and laid a box on the table. βÄ€Princess, I have a surprise for you,βÄù He said.  She rolled off the couch back and sauntered over. The pawed the box. It moved. She jumped back, βÄ€Moew,βÄù she cried.  A soft rumble came from the box.  Her human pulled out a small kitten.  How could he? Has he taken to dethroning me?  βÄ€This little guy was hurt and alone,βÄù her human said.βÄùHe needs our help. His name is Jesus, itβÄôs Spanish.βÄù  She saw the kitten was missing a paw. Remembering her first days in the veterinary clinic, where Leeland saved her, her heart softenedβÄΠ a little.  Over time, she learned to love the kitten as her own. Eventually, she realized that she could make room in her life for another, that maybe, just maybe, she was not Lord of AllβÄΠ 

***

 NowβÄΠ go do something! See you around,  

Darrell G. Wolfe


 Storyteller | Writer | Thinker | Consultant |¬†Multipotentialite

title: Short Story: The memory is the last to goβÄΠ
text:
Short Story: The memory is the last to goβÄΠ¬†





***





The morning fog crisped AhsβÄôs nose as it mixed with the eerie mists emanating from the mingling crowds. The scent of the river always felt clean but mysterious this time of year. As though it promised to one day reveal its secrets, held in one of the many 1940βÄôs cars at the bottom before people learned to stop driving over thin ice.¬†





He walked the edges of town, avoiding the largest concentration of people, barely aware of the purple clouds wafting from a couple sneaking a groping session behind the tree, mixing with the purple and green fog flowing from Eddie, the bank manager sitting across the street, doing his best not to appear to be watching them. The colors of every hue wafting from the people mixing mostly into ghastly greys in the waning fall crowds.¬†





He did his best to ignore the mists after years of it. The only way to keep his sanity intact. Eyes down, straight ahead. But you had to look up, look both ways, to cross the street. His eyes were drawn to a particularly dark charcoal grey puff, he knew before he finished his glance that it would be Hank.¬†





Sure enough, covered in dirt and three layers of Goodwill, there was Hank, fighting with a knot in his bicycle basket.





Approaching cautiously, you never knew what mood Hank would be in, βÄ€Hey there Soldier, can I lend a hand?βÄù





βÄ€Hmpf,βÄù Hank let out a more defeated than usual grunt. βÄ€Why not, IβÄôve been trying to get this damned knot untied for three hours.βÄù¬†





An exaggeration, it had probably been three minutes. But that was Hank, he also βÄ‰single-handedlyβÄô defeated an entire Vietnam platoon with nothing but a make-shift shank carved from the remains of a Tonkin Snub-nosed Monkey.





Ash silently took the knot from Hank, pulled out his pocket knife-





βÄ€Stop-βÄù Hank retorted. βÄ€ThatβÄôs my best rope!βÄù





βÄ€DonβÄôt worry old man,βÄù Ash said. βÄ€IβÄôm not going to cut it, just prying it loose.βÄù





Hank eyed him warily.¬†





βÄ€There,βÄù Ash said, a few moments later. βÄ€ItβÄôs all loose for you.βÄù He put his pocket knife in the Kevlar holster.¬†





Hank inspected the rope. βÄ€No frays,βÄù he mumbled. βÄ€Thanks.βÄù¬† With that, he promptly ignored Ash, as usual, and went back to whatever imperative task his three hundred plastic bags in the buggy behind his bike required.¬†





Ash left him to his project. Hank reminded him of his father. If he couldnβÄôt save Dad, or himself for that matter, the least he could do would be to show some kindness to one of their own kind.¬†





βÄ€How long,βÄù Ash let out audibly. As he walked the docks to his boat, images of himself running around the countryside with only half an awareness of his insanity raced through his mind. Like father like son. He shrugged, climbing aboard. The fog was heavier here but the mists of the crowds stayed behind with them.¬†





Letting loose the tie-downs, he then started the engines and set off up-river for home, ripples disappearing into the fogβÄΠ much like his memory, he feared.





***









A short story, by Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller

title: White NoiseβÄΠ reconsidered.
text:
Years ago, I wrote a novel, or, a lot of one. I got to 60,000 words +/- but it never felt right. The beginning felt right, the middle felt empty, the end forced. It never really became much except a seed of an idea.¬†




Scene One:


https://authorfun.blogspot.com/2016/10/distortion-sceneone.html?m=1





Then my wife died, and I shelved the whole project. IβÄôm fact, I shelved writing in general, creatively.¬†




ItβÄôs been stirring again, but I donβÄôt feel good inside moving on yet. I feel I owed it to myself, and my late wife, to finish the first novel. Even if itβÄôs just a cathartic exercise.¬†





My new wife, a fellow widow, has been encourime to tap into my creative fun side, so, today, on a whim, I thought, maybe I can use ChatGPT as a sounding board to workshop the novel and start it over, with a better, more comprehensive, and more ties together story structure.¬†






It was a great chat, I have a ton of new ideas, and it helped me see how the whole story could fit together more naturally and coherently.¬†





The chat from today:








//Begin





Chat Title:


Three-Act Story Structure.





User


Using the classic three act structure taught by km wyland at helping writers become authors.com what is a story structure





ChatGPT


The classic three-act structure, as taught by K.M. Weiland at Helping Writers Become Authors, is a common framework used in storytelling. It is a basic formula that helps writers organize their stories into a beginning, middle, and end.





Act 1: The Setup





The first act sets up the story and introduces the main character. This is where you establish the world of the story, the characterβÄôs goals, and the conflict they will face. This act usually ends with the βÄ€inciting incident,βÄù which is the event that sets the story in motion.





Act 2: The Confrontation





The second act is the longest and most complicated part of the story. This is where the character confronts the main conflict, faces challenges, and experiences setbacks. The character must overcome obstacles and work towards achieving their goal. This act ends with the βÄ€climax,βÄù which is the moment of maximum tension or conflict in the story.





Act 3: The Resolution





The final act is where the story resolves. The character confronts the final conflict and achieves their goal, or they fail and must deal with the consequences. This act ends with the βÄ€resolution,βÄù which is the conclusion of the story.





Overall, the three-act structure is a useful tool for organizing a story and keeping it on track. By following this structure, writers can create a cohesive and satisfying narrative that engages readers from beginning to end.

















User


I know that my story story starts with a disabled veteran who had gone through some experiments and is seeing things what he doesnβÄôt realize at first is that the things heβÄôs seeing are actually real spirits of the spirit world. He is eventually attacked by the people who did the experiments in attempt to keep them quiet. I think the story ultimately resolves when he meets the doctor in charge of the experiment and he is able to make peace with the spirit world whereas the doctor is not. The doctor ends up in an insane asylum and the veteran ends up working with other experimentes to help them unlock their powers. What would a story structure look like around this concept and what kinds of things might be missing from The Middle.





ChatGPT


Based on the concept you have provided, here is a possible structure for your story, using the classic three-act structure:





Act 1: The Setup





Introduction of the disabled veteran and his experiences with the experiments


Establishment of the spirit world and the veteranβÄôs interactions with it


The inciting incident: the veteran is attacked by the people who conducted the experiments


Act 2: The Confrontation





The veteran is on the run, trying to uncover the truth about the experiments and why he was targeted


He meets other people who have undergone similar experiments and discovers their unique abilities


The veteranβÄôs powers grow stronger as he becomes more in tune with the spirit world


The climax: the veteran confronts the doctor in charge of the experiments and must make a choice about his own destiny and the fate of the other experimentees


Act 3: The Resolution





The veteran comes to terms with his powers and makes peace with the spirit world


The doctor is committed to an insane asylum, unable to cope with the consequences of his actions


The veteran begins to work with the other experimentees, helping them to control their abilities and navigate the spirit world


In terms of what might be missing from the middle, it could be helpful to consider specific obstacles and challenges that the veteran faces as he tries to uncover the truth and gain control of his powers. These could include encounters with other entities in the spirit world, conflicts with other experimentees who have different goals or motivations, and further attempts by the people who conducted the experiments to stop him. Additionally, the middle could benefit from moments of reflection and character development, as the veteran comes to terms with his experiences and learns to trust himself and his powers.

















User


Exploring Act 1. A female reporter, Destiny, has been tracking down the edges of the story for a few years. She may even be the reason itβÄôs become dangerous to leave the experimentees alive? Maybe. She meets our veteran, Ash, in a small town under the pretense of doing a story on small towns in America, but sheβÄôs actually there because he and one other man, a homeless man named Chester, were experimentees. But Ash doesnβÄôt remember being a test subject. He remembers volunteering for a military program, that led to his medical discharge, but his memory of the actual events is fuzzy, and he thinks itβÄôs resulted in hallucinations and nightmares. All he wants is to be βÄ€normalβÄù. What he doesnβÄôt realize yet, is that the hallucinations are actually beings from another dimension, that some religious people call spirits, and the dreams are actually memories trying to surface from the experiment. He uses prescription drugs to dull these affects, ineffectively.¬†


Brainstorming with me, What types of events could take place in the first act?





ChatGPT


Here are some possible events that could take place in the first act of your story:





DestinyβÄôs investigation: We see Destiny doing research, interviewing people, and gathering information about the experiments and their participants. She may uncover clues that point her in AshβÄôs direction.


AshβÄôs daily life: We get a glimpse of AshβÄôs current life, which is characterized by isolation, fear, and a desire to be βÄ€normalβÄù. We see how his experiences with the experiments have affected him, and how he copes with his symptoms.


Ash and DestinyβÄôs initial meeting: They meet in the small town where Ash lives, and strike up a conversation. Ash is initially guarded, but something about Destiny intrigues him. Destiny is friendly and curious, but is also hiding her true motives for being there.


ChesterβÄôs appearance: Chester, the other experimentee, makes an appearance. He may be homeless, but heβÄôs streetwise and perceptive. He recognizes something in Ash that suggests they have a connection, and he starts asking questions.


The first encounter with the spirit world: Ash has a vivid hallucination or dream that involves a spirit. At first he dismisses it as a hallucination, but the experience lingers with him and starts to raise questions.


AshβÄôs memories start to resurface: Ash starts to have brief flashes of memory, which are disorienting and confusing. He may try to ignore them at first, but they become more persistent and troubling.














User


Typically, the inciting event takes place early, something that shakes the protagonist from his current world and begins foreshadowing the story world. The first plot point takes place at the end of Act 1, forcing the protagonist beyond the point of no return. IβÄôm my original draft, I toyed with an assassination attempt on AshβÄôs life that forces him to find out whatβÄôs happening. However, while the scene was fascinating, it left me stuck for βÄ€but now whatβÄù? in Act 2.¬†





What kinds of inciting events and plot points could we consider for Act 1 with Ash as a primary protagonist?





ChatGPT


Here are some potential inciting events and plot points for Act 1 with Ash as the primary protagonist:





The discovery of ChesterβÄôs death: Ash discovers that Chester has been killed, and starts to realize that there may be people trying to cover up their involvement in the experiments. This could spur him to take action and start investigating what happened to him and Chester.


AshβÄôs first encounter with the people who did the experiments: Ash is confronted by the people who conducted the experiments, who may be trying to silence him or intimidate him into keeping quiet. This could be a scary and disorienting experience for him, and could motivate him to seek answers.


A breakthrough in AshβÄôs memories: Ash has a vivid dream or flashback that gives him a significant piece of information about what happened to him during the experiments. This could be a turning point for him, as he realizes that his memories may be the key to understanding whatβÄôs going on.


Destiny reveals her true intentions: Ash discovers that Destiny is actually a reporter investigating the experiments, and that sheβÄôs been keeping her true intentions hidden from him. This could create a sense of betrayal and mistrust between them, while also pushing Ash to take action and help her uncover the truth.


The emergence of a new spirit: Ash has a powerful encounter with a spirit that seems different from any heβÄôs seen before. This could be a sign that something has changed, and that the spirit world may be more dangerous or unpredictable than he initially thought.


Ash is forced to make a choice: Ash is presented with a difficult decision, such as whether to trust Destiny or go it alone, whether to confront the people behind the experiments or keep his head down, or whether to continue using drugs to dull his symptoms or try to confront them head-on. This choice could have significant consequences for the rest of the story.














User


All but the last point were included in my original draft, which tells me I was going in the right direction, but your nuances gave me new ideas for those scenes.¬†





On the last bullet point, that could give me the middle Act 2 you suggested further back in this dialogue. What kind of decision could he be facing? It sounds like maybe the drugs dulling things may be a key factor. What if Destiny replaced his drugs with placebos to force him awake, out of desperation both to get her story, and the truth, but also because sheβÄôs falling for him and she wants to save him?





ChatGPT


ThatβÄôs an interesting idea! Here are a few possible decisions that Ash could be facing in Act 1 or Act 2:





Whether to continue using the drugs or try to face his symptoms without them. This could be a difficult decision for Ash, as the drugs may be the only way he can function in his day-to-day life, but theyβÄôre also preventing him from fully understanding whatβÄôs happening to him. DestinyβÄôs decision to replace his drugs with placebos could be the catalyst that forces Ash to confront this choice head-on.


Whether to trust Destiny or go it alone. As Ash begins to work with Destiny to uncover the truth about the experiments, he may start to develop feelings for her. But if he finds out that sheβÄôs been deceiving him by replacing his drugs with placebos, he may begin to question her motives and wonder if he can trust her. This could lead him to choose between working with her to uncover the truth or going it alone.


Whether to confront the people behind the experiments or keep his head down. As Ash gets closer to the truth, he may start to feel like heβÄôs in over his head. He may be torn between trying to take down the people who did the experiments, and trying to protect himself and his loved ones from their retribution. This could be a difficult decision for Ash, as it may require him to put himself in danger in order to do the right thing.


Whether to confront his memories or try to suppress them. As Ash starts to remember more about the experiments, he may begin to feel overwhelmed by the traumatic memories. He may have to decide whether to try to suppress these memories in order to preserve his mental health, or whether to confront them head-on in order to uncover the truth. This could be a difficult decision for Ash, as it may require him to confront painful and uncomfortable memories in order to get to the bottom of what happened to him.














User


Being former special forces, USAF, he has both the former drive to stare down this challenge, but also the defeated ego from years of what he thinks is PTSD to doubt his ability to even live a lifeβÄΠ If he realizes it may not be PTSD, and heβÄôs not actually going crazy, this may force him to take the plunge into the adventure!





ChatGPT


Yes, that could be a powerful motivator for Ash to finally take action and confront the truth about his experiences. If he realizes that what heβÄôs been experiencing is not just PTSD, but something much more complex and sinister, it could give him the push he needs to take on this new challenge.





Additionally, the fact that Ash is a former special forces member could give him a unique set of skills and resources that he can use to his advantage in the story. Perhaps he has contacts in the military or the intelligence community that could help him uncover information about the experiments, or maybe he has specialized training that will come in handy as he faces off against the people responsible for the experiments.





Ultimately, the decision to confront his past and seek the truth could be a turning point for AshβÄôs character. It could force him to reevaluate his entire identity and what he thought he knew about himself, and it could be the key to unlocking his true potential as a protagonist.

















User


Now. What if Destiny had her own POV in the story , with her own three act structureβÄΠ What would her inciting event and first plot point be, that coincide but either nuance or contrast AshβÄôs?





ChatGPT


ThatβÄôs an interesting idea! If Destiny had her own POV and three act structure, her inciting event could be when she first learns about the experiments and becomes intrigued by them. Maybe she comes across a small mention of the experiments in a news article or hears about them from a source, and she becomes determined to uncover the truth.





Her first plot point could be when she meets Ash and realizes that he is a key piece of the puzzle. Maybe she initially approaches him under the guise of doing a story on small towns in America, but she quickly realizes that he has a deeper connection to the experiments. This realization could force her to make a choice: continue down the safe path of doing a standard human interest story or pursue the risky path of investigating the experiments and potentially putting herself in danger.





Alternatively, her first plot point could be when she faces pushback from her editor or her colleagues for pursuing the story. Maybe they think itβÄôs too risky or too niche, and they want her to focus on more mainstream topics. This pushback could force her to make a choice: abandon the story and stick to safer topics, or continue pursuing the story at all costs.





By having DestinyβÄôs own POV and three act structure, you could create a rich, layered story that explores the experiences of both protagonists in depth. It could also allow for interesting contrasts and comparisons between their perspectives and motivations.





βÄ™

title: Major Theron βÄ€AshβÄù Ashland, USAF Spec Ops, Special Tactics Unit
text:
Theron Ashland¬†


Major Theron βÄ€AshβÄù Ashland, USAF Spec Ops, Special Tactics Unit


WeβÄôre Air Force Special Tactics, and our motto is βÄ€First ThereβÄΠThat Others May Live.βÄù¬†







Birth Date: xx/xx/1978, in Moon-Wolf Hollow, Idaho, on the family tree-farm, which is hundreds of acres of wooded hills, streams, and a small lake.



His father was a distant, hard-working, lumberman. The father died when Ash was very young.¬†


His mother was kind but broken by the loss of his father. Widowhood took root in her life as an identity, and she lived in the past in a way that left Theron taken care of physically, but emotionally unsupported. He developed an attachment style of Avoidant, so that he would never burden his mother with his emotions. She too died when Ash was just 18, and he spent the year getting his familyβÄôs affairs in order, including leaving the family property in Trust, deeded to an LLC, which was managed by a friend of the family, including tree harvesting.








Enrolled at University of Idaho in 1997 at 19 - Ash majored in Organizational Leadership. But at the beginning of his fourth year, 9/11 happened and he dropped everything to enlist in the USAF the next day.




USAF Enlistment: 09/12/2001, Age at Enlistment: 23



He became a sniper in the USAF Spec Ops, Special Tactics Unit, working his way through the Enlisted Ranks.¬†


Pouring himself into each assignment was his way of running from his own past. He handled the family property remotely through calls, and never went home on leave.








Injured on Duty: xx/xx/2010, Age at Injury: 33



After being injured (shot?) badly enough to require a lengthy hospital stay and rehab, Ash was restless and ended up using his time to complete the BA he left behind ten years earlier. Stacking summer and winter school on top of the maximum units the school would allow, he used all the time he wasnβÄôt in Physical Therapy on school.¬†¬†


Temporary Disability Retired List (TDRL): 2010-2011 During which he completed a BA (BOLD) and MBA program, then joined Officer Training School (OTS).





After his return to service, now as an Officer, Ash climbed to the rank of Major between 2011-2018.



During this time, his Leadership as an Officer with deep field experience became invaluable to his superiors, and a point of frustration to his peers, which his advice ran counter to theirs, which was always.





Medically Discharged: xx/xx/2018 at 40-yearβÄôs old.



After an injury achieved in the field under suspicious circumstances, Ash was medically discharged at 40-yearβÄôs old. He went home to Moon-Wolf Hollow, Idaho, USA - Where he has been quietly living on his family property, alone, a valued member of the town, but truly known by very few.



Home Life:



He runs a small business selling hand-crafted woodwork, and he is a part-time weapons instructor for the local shooting club.¬†


Ash keeps to himself, never attends parties, rarely comes to town for parades or events, especially if tourists are in town.


Ash does attend a local church, but only as a socially expected norm for the region. He is an admitted agnostic when pressed.¬†


His only real friend in town is the local sheriff, a retired Army veteran who understands AshβÄôs need to be alone.


He also attends a wounded warriors recovery group once a month.¬†


His PTSD symptoms come and go, some months worse than others.¬†


When he was first discharged, he was prescribed strong narcotics, and those helped him through the early days. Eventually, just before his full discharge, they found a cocktail that worked well enough.¬†


His local doctor, Sam?XXX, who played the dual role of small-town physician and mental health therapist, admitted that Idaho was behind the times on narcotics, and that Marijuana was showing a lot of good results in studies for PTSD. He went across the boarder to WA to get it for a while, but eventually obtained the necessary materials to grow his own. The local Sheriff backed this decision, and kept the feds away. Luckily, they left small towns like this alone most of the time, out of the spotlight.



This is the state we find him, at a coffee shop, where he is having a particular over-powering PTSD vision, one he hasnβÄôt had at this level since his first days in the hospital after he was found and recovered unconscious.







Etymology


Theron: βÄ€ΈΗΈΖœ¹Έ·ΈΩΈΫβÄù (therion) - Wild Animal, Beast, Untamed, by extension, Hunter


Ashland: An estate where Ash trees propagate.





MyerβÄôs Briggs/16-Personalities Type:


Commander: ENTJ-A / ENTJ-T


Bold, imaginative and strong-willed leaders, always finding a way βÄ™ or making one.





Clifton Strengths:




Strategic - ENTJs are known for their ability to quickly identify patterns and various possibilities in a given situation.


Command - This strength aligns with the ENTJβÄôs natural leadership qualities and their ability to take control of a situation and make decisions.


Achiever - Their driven nature to accomplish goals and constant need for achievement resonates well with this strength.


Focus - ENTJs typically have the ability to set clear goals and remain on track towards achieving them.


Significance - They often seek to make a big impact and want their work and presence to be of significant value.




Attachment Style:


Avoidant


Unhealthy Relationships, Codependent, May be an Addict.


IβÄôm OK, YouβÄôre NOT OK.


I can self regulate


I cannot bring my needs to anyone, ever.


Because I cannot bring my needs to anyone, and some needs require others, I have learned to shut my needs down to the point I may not know they exist. You will have a difficult time feeling connected to me.

title: Write Practice 11/27/2023
text:
I died last week.




Now here I am, re-embodied, asking βÄ€What do I do now?βÄù¬†





My death was brief, so IβÄôm told. I was only dead for sixty seconds. It didnβÄôt feel like sixty seconds to me, it felt like an eternity. I met people I once knew. I met people I hadnβÄôt believed really existed. I met Jesus, and Paul. I also met King Arthur.¬†





ItβÄôs all a bit fuzzy now, the realness of it all is fading. I was sent back for a purpose, the question is, what is that purpose? I suppose that is my mission now, to find my purpose.
