title: Yes/And, Yes/But, No/And, and No/But: Narrative Tools for Dynamic Storytelling
text:
* I have heard about these concepts in many ways, from teachers of creative writing to a local improv class for acting. I had a longer conversation with ChatGPT trying to bring it all back to memory and try to remind myself how the techniques are supposed to work, so I can start working on a story. After my back and forth, I asked ChatGPT to summarize our discussion in the form of an article. Here is the article.¬†I'm also going to go ahead and put the start of a rough sketch for the opening of the scene to show you why I stopped to have this reminder session.¬†This YouTube video gave me the idea to start drafting in a new way, which is akin to Brandon Sanderson's version of Outlining in his BYU lectures.
  Don't Give Up On Your First Draft by 
The Second Story

  
  
  
  
  ChatGPT Summary:Reminder -- Yes/And, Yes/But, No/And, and No/But: Narrative Tools for
Dynamic Storytelling

Conflict and collaboration are the beating heart of
compelling storytelling. Writers often use structured approaches to dialogue,
action, and character dynamics to keep their narratives engaging. Four
foundational tools in this toolbox are Yes/And, Yes/But, No/And,
and No/But. Each approach creates unique rhythms in a storyΟΔβ²§β³Δs
progression, balancing tension, resolution, and stakes. LetΟΔβ²§β³Δs explore these
methods, their narrative implications, and how they can be used holistically
within a story.







1. Yes/And: Momentum and Expansion

Yes/And accepts an idea or action and builds on it,
encouraging collaboration and the organic expansion of the storyΟΔβ²§β³Δs world or
character arcs. This approach moves the narrative forward with a sense of
discovery and cooperation.

Example:

Two explorers discover a strange artifact in a cave.

Explorer 1: ΟΔβ²§≈™This artifact feels warm. ItΟΔβ²§β³Δs alive!ΟΔβ²§¬ù Explorer
2: ΟΔβ²§≈™Yes, and the symbols on it match the ones we found in the ruins. We might
be onto something big.ΟΔβ²§¬ù


 Result:
     The conversation builds momentum. The characters deepen their shared
     understanding of the artifact, leading to further exploration and raising
     questions about its purpose and origin.


Use in Stories:


 Ideal
     for world-building, where details can grow naturally.
 Strengthens
     relationships between characters through mutual cooperation.
 Creates
     a sense of progression and discovery without immediate conflict.








2. Yes/But: Complication and Partial Progress

Yes/But accepts an idea but introduces a limitation
or complication. It allows partial success while keeping the stakes high,
creating nuanced challenges for characters.

Example:

A hero seeks permission from the council to lead an army
against invaders.

Council Leader: ΟΔβ²§≈™Yes, you can lead the army, but youΟΔβ²§β³Δll have
to rally the factions yourselfΟΔβ²§βÄùand theyΟΔβ²§β³Δre not easy to persuade.ΟΔβ²§¬ù


 Result:
     The hero gains an opportunity but faces a new challenge: uniting fractured
     groups with conflicting interests. This adds layers of conflict while
     enabling forward motion.


Use in Stories:


 Adds
     tension without halting the narrative.
 Creates
     opportunities for character growth as they overcome obstacles.
 Balances
     progress with challenges, maintaining reader engagement.








3. No/And: Escalation and Surprise

No/And denies a characterΟΔβ²§β³Δs goal or expectation while
escalating the stakes. This approach often creates dramatic twists, forcing
characters into crisis or unexpected decisions.

Example:

A detective confronts a suspect with evidence of their
guilt.

Suspect: ΟΔβ²§≈™No, I didnΟΔβ²§β³Δt kill him, and if you keep digging,
youΟΔβ²§β³Δll find out something you wish you hadnΟΔβ²§β³Δt.ΟΔβ²§¬ù


 Result:
     The suspectΟΔβ²§β³Δs denial heightens the mystery and tension. The additional
     threat raises stakes, propelling the detective deeper into dangerous
     territory.


Use in Stories:


 Introduces
     unforeseen complications or twists.
 Increases
     stakes, pushing characters out of their comfort zones.
 Perfect
     for moments of high tension, such as confrontations or turning points.








4. No/But: Deflection and Obstacle

No/But rejects an idea or action while offering an
alternative path. It creates conflict by shutting down the initial proposal but
provides a new direction for the narrative.

Example:

A protagonist begs their partner to flee a war-torn city.

Partner: ΟΔβ²§≈™No, I wonΟΔβ²§β³Δt leave, but IΟΔβ²§β³Δll help you convince
others to evacuate before itΟΔβ²§β³Δs too late.ΟΔβ²§¬ù


 Result:
     The partnerΟΔβ²§β³Δs refusal creates tension but also introduces an alternative
     that deepens the emotional stakes. The protagonist must navigate the
     partnerΟΔβ²§β³Δs priorities while achieving their goal.


Use in Stories:


 Adds
     realism by reflecting compromise or partial resistance.
 Develops
     complex relationships between characters.
 Keeps
     the story dynamic by forcing adjustments to plans.








Using These Approaches Holistically

Effective storytelling often involves weaving these
techniques together, alternating between momentum, complication, escalation,
and deflection to create a dynamic and layered narrative. HereΟΔβ²§β³Δs how they can
work in harmony:


 Establish
     the World with Yes/And: Use this approach early in the story to build
     the setting, relationships, and stakes. Create a foundation where
     characters and their goals are clearly understood.
 Introduce
     Challenges with Yes/But: Once the groundwork is laid, complicate the
     story by introducing obstacles that force the characters to adapt and
     grow.
 Escalate
     Tension with No/And: As the narrative peaks, use this method to
     amplify stakes and force characters into crisis, driving the story toward
     its climax.
 Resolve
     with No/But: In the resolution, employ this approach to reflect the
     bittersweet nature of many endings. Characters may not achieve their exact
     goals, but they find alternative paths that lead to growth or closure.








Conclusion

Yes/And, Yes/But, No/And, and No/But are versatile tools
that allow writers to balance progress and conflict. By understanding and
combining these methods, storytellers can create narratives that feel organic,
engaging, and emotionally resonant. Whether youΟΔβ²§β³Δre writing a sweeping epic or a
tight character-driven drama, these approaches provide a structured yet
flexible framework for dynamic storytelling.

¬†Scenes vs SequelsK.M. Weiland discusses "Scenes and Sequels" as the fundamental
building blocks of storytelling structure. Here's a summary of her approach:Scenes
 Goal: The character actively pursues a
     specific, concrete goal.
 Conflict: Obstacles arise that prevent the
     character from easily achieving the goal.
 Disaster: The scene ends with the character's
     efforts failing in some way, propelling the story forward by raising
     stakes and questions.
Sequels










 Reaction: The character processes the disaster
     emotionally and mentally, reflecting on the consequences.
 Dilemma: The character faces a choice or
     crossroads caused by the disaster.
 Decision: The character makes a choice that
     leads to their next course of action, setting up the goal for the next
     scene.
Rough Sketch Scene:Scene Hook/Grounded

Summary: I want to show Ash in his current state,
hook the reader, and give him a SMALL want, but one that also serves to flush
out his current state. -- Ash want wants to get his coffee and make it to the
corner chair without having to interact too often or look like heΟΔβ²§β³Δs as unstable
as he feels.

Beat 1 - No (Coffee/Chair/Avoid people) / And (Ned finds him)

Ash is in a small rural Idaho town, looking out the window,
hallucinating, suffering from PTSD. His interactions with (and avoidance of)
the townspeople, whoΟΔβ²§β³Δve known him since he was a boy except for the years he
was away in the military doing things he canΟΔβ²§β³Δt talk about because they are
classified, show a man whoΟΔβ²§β³Δs loved by the people but also pitied. His life was
sad in heartbreaking ways, and nobody can do anything for him but to love him
when possible, and give him space as needed. ItΟΔβ²§β³Δs a good town full of good
people, but they believe in a lot of conspiracy theory nonsense, and theyΟΔβ²§β³Δre
scared of anything they donΟΔβ²§β³Δt understand. This scene needs some kind of an arch
or goal. The opening hallucination is a great hook, but what does Ash want
(small wants, immediate wants) in this scene that can give it a start, middle,
and end? He wants to get his coffee and make it to the corner chair without
having to interact too often or look like heΟΔβ²§β³Δs as unstable as he feels. OooooΟΔβ²§¬Π
thatΟΔβ²§β³Δs a cool scene. Okay, so? What happens. Does he get his coffee?

No, butΟΔβ²§¬Π 

Scene opens: Ash is staring out the window, he sees shadows
move across the street and reaches for his gun, but there is no gun. There is
however a tape measure on his belt that is now in his hand. He rushes with embarrassment,
and looks around to see if anyone noticed. A few looks but the room isnΟΔβ²§β³Δt
staring at him, so he is fine, everything is fine, ΟΔβ²§≈™IΟΔβ²§β³Δm not crazyΟΔβ²§¬ùΟΔβ²§¬Π 

He practices his breathing techniques and embodiment
practices, he starts observing the last leaves of autumn fall from the trees,
one lands in a pumpkin and burns. As he watches the people passing by, does he
wonder anything about them? Does he try to remember what life was like before
he was in this condition? 

A ΟΔβ²§≈™NedΟΔβ²§¬ù like character sees him through the window and
starts waving and rushing over. Ash pretends not to see him, turns and asks if
his coffee is ready yet. The bariste chides him to wait his turn, but in a
friendly way. Ash picks something up off the rack to read the label just to look
distracted. The bell on the door rings and creaks as it opens. Ned makes a
beeline for Ash.

Ned: Ash. 

*Ignored*

Ned: Ash.

*Ignored*

Ned: Hey buddy, (gently taps him on the shoulder).

Ash feels his back and neck tense, and a rush of prickles
through his skin. But he forces a smile and turns around, then pulls his ear
phones out, he always keeps them in, but usually nothing is playing, it simply
keeps the intrusive noises down. But it makes a great excuse to pretend you
didnΟΔβ²§β³Δt hear someone.

Ash: (turning around and pulling one ear bud out), Hi Ned.
DidnΟΔβ²§β³Δt see you there. 

Ned: You are always in your head. So listen, (Ned rushes
before Ash can run away), weΟΔβ²§β³Δre doing that fundraiser again and you havenΟΔβ²§β³Δt
confirmed your table yet this year.

Ash: Is that coming up already?

Ned: The ten emails this week werenΟΔβ²§β³Δt enough to remind you?

Ash: I donΟΔβ²§β³Δt read emails. I only have that email address for
signing into my VA benefits portal. 

Ned: You are an odd one. YouΟΔβ²§β³Δre coming right? I can put you
down for a table?

Ash: (takes a deep breath and sighs into accepting his
membership in this town), ΟΔβ²§≈™You have been a persistent one ever since you got
your first Boy Scout popcorn tin. Sure, IΟΔβ²§β³Δll be there.ΟΔβ²§¬ù 

Ned: Great, thanks, everyone loves your stuff.

*AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs name is called by the Bariste*

Ash: Sure ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ I gotta go buddy. *points at the pick up
counter*

Ned: Awesome, See you then. *Ned rushes off to pester
another late responder across the cafΟÉ¬©*

Beat 2 - Yes (Coffee) / But No (Chair) Interrupted on the way

Ash grabs his coffee and starts walking to his corner, but as
a lady passes by holding a small yippy dog[DGW1]¬†. The dog goes from happy and yippy to
aggressive as it passes Ash. It starts barking at Ash. Ash can see the demon
entity on the lady and another like it on the dog, and they both are staring at
him with hate in their eyes, and the dog is reacting to it. The lady is oblivious,
but simultaneously wary of Ash and embarrassed by her dogs behavior. Just as
the lady is finally moving on, the dog leaps out of her arms and starts nipping
at AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs pant leg. He shakes it off and grabs it by the scruff of the neck and
hands it to the lady without a word. She leaves to put the dog in a time-out in
her car. Ash begins to make a bee-line for the chair in the corner. 

Beat 3 - Yes (Coffee) Yes (Chair) / But No (more intrusions) A man comes to
sit and chat

(yes) Ash sits in the chair and breathes a sigh of relief. He
made it to his chair, passed the interruptions, and he has his coffee. Ash
surveys the rooms, showing that he is wary of any environment, keeping his back
to the corner, observing all points of entry, watching peopleΟΔβ²§β³Δs reactions while
trying to look uninterested. After practicing his embodiment exercises, and
putting aside a few observations of other spirit creatures in the room, he
picks up the paper or book to read. 

(but) A man sits down in the chair next to Ash, with a small
coffee table in between for setting drinks. Ash notices from the corner of his
eye that the man is looking directly at him, but pretends not to notice. But
the manΟΔβ²§β³Δs stare never wavers or shifts, so Ash finally puts the book in his
lap, holding the place with his thumb (but also positioning the book in such a
way as to use it as a weapon), and matches the manΟΔβ²§β³Δs stare eye to eye. 

Neither speak for what seems like an eternity, and Ash
raises an eyebrow, finally closing the book fully, and gripping it tighter,
ready for a full fight. The man nods to himself, and sits back a little in his
chair. Both men take a deep breath and relax a titch. The man finally speaks.

Man: I wasnΟΔβ²§β³Δt sure it was you, but it is you. Unbelievable. 

Ash: Do we know each other? My memory isnΟΔβ²§β³Δt what it used to
be, but I donΟΔβ²§β³Δt believe weΟΔβ²§β³Δve met. 

Some conversation ensues that makes it seem the man wanted
to tell Ash something, but not in this busy roomΟΔβ²§¬Π He will send an invitation
later. Or maybe he tells Ash where he is staying? 

There is a commotion outside, and Ash stands up, taking a
few steps away to peak out the window to see whatΟΔβ²§β³Δs happening. When he turns back
to the chairs to tell the man heΟΔβ²§β³Δs not interested, the man is gone, but heΟΔβ²§β³Δs
left his card on AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs seat. It appears that he is (something boring, but
coded? Something interesting? What?)ΟΔβ²§¬Π. 

Scene breaks, Ash leaves the cafΟÉ¬©. 

¬†

Sequel (Reaction Dilemma Decision) 

Ned but The Business Card? Ash left the cafΟÉ¬© a few hours
ago, frustrated that the outing had gone so poorly. He is in his workshop,
drinking liquor to numb his nerves. He pitters around the workshop trying to
see what product he has available for the winter market and what item he can
donate to the fundraiser. Unlike other makers, his word craft isnΟΔβ²§β³Δt something
he can throw together. He can carve a few small items in an afternoon, for
funsies, but it takes him weeks for a table or chair. 

All the while, the business card sits on the shelf calling
to him. He keeps trying to ignore it, his attention drawn back repeatedly,
until he finally picks it up and sits in one of his rocking chairs to read it,
looking for clues or queues. Is this a real card? Is it coded in some way? Is
there a hidden message? Is this just a sales gimmick to get him to buy into
some new Ponzi scheme? But there was something about the man, wasnΟΔβ²§β³Δt there? A
hint of something just out of the reach of AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs memory, like the ghost of a
memory? Could this guy be one of his old contacts? Could he be related to an
old contact? Could this actually be a set up to take Ash down for an old
grudge? But would that even make sense? ItΟΔβ²§β³Δs been way too long, heΟΔβ²§β³Δs been out
of the game for way too long. Could anyone alive today still care what he did
in his former occupation? Some might, especially a son or daughter, maybe. 

This could be innocuous sales scheme, an old acquaintance,
or a genuine threat to his life. While remote, the possibility exists. If he
doesnΟΔβ²§β³Δt come to meet the man, the man or his associates could show up when Ash
isnΟΔβ²§β³Δt looking or ready. It would be better to face the issue head on. He
decides to check out the guyΟΔβ²§β³Δs hotel room, do some recon before agreeing to
meet.

¬†











¬†[DGW1]A
mix between a Chihuahua and a Poodle is commonly called a "Chipoo."
Sometimes it's also referred to as a Choodle, Poochi, or Chi-Poo. These
designer dogs combine characteristics from both parent breeds, leading to a
small, active, and often quite intelligent pet.

¬†










I hope you enjoyed that random tidbit. Darrell

title: My opening thoughtsβÄΠ I have no idea how I will use Buy Me A Coffee
text:
My opening thoughtsβÄΠ I have no idea how I will use Buy Me A Coffee


In General, I know what marketing people do with tools like βÄ€Buy Me A CoffeeβÄù and Patreon and others. They create a false sense of scarcity and urgency by limiting certain content to certain people who can pay for the upgrades stuff.


The very thought of doing something like that makes me want to die inside. I would rather have a universe where all our needs our met, nobody has too much or too little, and we just do what we love and our motivation is to enjoy sharing what we love with our kindred spirits.


I also come from a unique eccentric mix of Judeo-Christian traditions, which have caused me to deconstruct the US-American Churchianity of my upbringing and seek out The Way of Rabbi Yeshua (who is way more interesting than my Church told me he was). As a result, my ethic is to share freely, widely, and openly. However, if IβÄôm working so many hours to keep lights and food on that I canβÄôt write, I have nothing to share.


So here is my opening thought about starting this Buy Me Coffee channelβÄΠ. 


Support My Work: If you want to support my work so I can keep doing more, buy my work on Amazon/Kindle/Audible (when it becomes available there, there is nothing there today, 01/25/2025), or Buy Me A Coffee.


My goal: Subject to change, you will never have to pay me anything to have access to my content outside of AmazonβÄôs ecosystem. So if you want to support me outside that system, Buy Me A Coffee.




Writing - Where you can find me


I write blogs, fiction, and academic articles. I will post those on the various channels for which they are appropriate.




Blog - www.DarrellWolfe.com - My most random thoughts of the moment.




Blog - www.NoHiding.Faith - All my biblical studies writing goes here.




Fiction -




Darrellwolfe.com - Sometimes post rough drafts and short stories and practice writing stuff on my personal blog, www.DarrellWolfe.com




Royal Road - DarrellWolfeβÄôs Profile | Royal Road




Wattpad - Darrell G. Wolfe (D.G. Wolfe) (@darrellwolfe) - Wattpad




Good Reads - I donβÄôt see how to use Good Reads to post anything, but if thatβÄôs possible I have a profile there: https://www.goodreads.com/darrellgwolfe




Amazon/Audible - ($) - This is the only avenue other than Buy Me Coffee where I plan to add a price tag to my work.








Community - Discord


Storytellers - https://discord.gg/6UK5Txeq


I just started a discord server where we can start to build whatever we want there. Maybe separate channels for fiction, biblical studies, exvangelicals, etc. I dontβÄô know, letβÄôs figure it out together.




What else should I be considering? I anticipate nobody will actually read this post, or it will be years from now. But I wanted to put it out into the ether and let it marinate while I work on a complete re-write of my first novel for the fourth time, but this time with new tools and a reasonable expectation of putting something out worth reading. We shall see.




Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller 





    



Subscribe Now - Pursue a No Hiding Lifestyle¬†









 Shalom: Live Long and Prosper!

Darrell Wolfe (DG Wolfe)


Storyteller | Writer | Thinker | Consultant @¬†DarrellWolfe.com  Clifton StrengthsFinder: Intellection, Learner,¬†Ideation,¬†Achiever, Input 16Personalities (Myers-Briggs Type): INFJ

title: White Noise - Distortion (re-write #4) Beat Sheet - Outline - Progress Update 01/26/2025
text:
White Noise - Distortion (re-write #4) Beat Sheet - Outline - Progress Update 01/26/2025


This is an updated draft of my new re-write, which I hope will land me on a completed novel this time.



See¬†DarrellβÄôs Writing Plan for 2025 ~ Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller¬†for my current writing plan and the background behind this new attempt.


¬†



¬†


Distortion (Outline Level Rough Draft)


A White Noise Story


Rewrite Notes



Ο²¬Ζ¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬† In the actual writing of this after outlining, weave in spiritual things he sees, and make them inconvenient for him.



Ο²¬Ζ¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬† At core, this is an information plot, a romance plot, an adventure plot? 



Ο²¬Ζ¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬† I think we can write BriarΟΔβ²§β³Δs arch, but at first we only see snippets of his arch via text messages he is sending Thaddeaus. We donΟΔβ²§β³Δt actually see his POV for a while until weΟΔβ²§β³Δre fully invested in Ash and Destiny. However, I want Briar to be every bit as real, owning his own wants, as they are. We want to almost want him to get his goals, or at least we can feel how he got where he is. 



Ο²¬Ζ¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬† We seem to be settling on Ash not wanting to seem crazy to others. Somehow when we write the scenes, we need to paint both the chaos of his inner world and how little that actually escapes into otherΟΔβ²§β³Δs view. But it does escape, and people do see something is odd about him, and many give him space because of it, but also pity. They know heΟΔβ²§β³Δs struggling and many rumors have spread about the extent of it. They do see the cracks in his sanity, but they do not see the whirlwind of chaos inside of him. They also donΟΔβ²§β³Δt know specifically that he is hallucinating. If he knew they knew that, he would be mortified. But they know he sees things, or maybe remembers things? They have suspicions about it. They assume itΟΔβ²§β³Δs flash backs from the military, or his momΟΔβ²§β³Δs death. Which he was present for before leaving for the military? Maybe a car crash? Maybe a murder? Not sure. 


¬†


Captain (retired) Theron¬†βÄ€AshβÄù Ashland, USAF Spec Ops


Captain Theron βÄ€AshβÄù Ashland, USAF Spec Ops. ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ I think I later realized this would be Colonel or something? IΟΔβ²§β³Δll have to check my notes. I am not sure it matters anymore. 


¬†


Briar Texts as epigraphs before AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs POVs


01/18/2025


HOOK


Scene Hook/Grounded


Goal: I want to show Ash in his current state, hook the reader, and give him a SMALL want, but one that also serves to flush out his current state. βÄ™ Ash wants wants to get his coffee and make it to the corner chair without having to interact too often or look like heΟΔβ²§β³Δs as unstable as he feels.


Beat 1 - No (Coffee/Chair/Avoid people) / And (Ned finds him)


Ash is in a small rural Idaho town, looking out the window, hallucinating, suffering from PTSD. 


His interactions with (and avoidance of) the townspeople, whoΟΔβ²§β³Δve known him since he was a boy except for the years he was away in the military doing things he canΟΔβ²§β³Δt talk about because they are classified, show a man whoΟΔβ²§β³Δs loved by the people but also pitied. His life was sad in heartbreaking ways, and nobody can do anything for him but to love him when possible, and give him space as needed. ItΟΔβ²§β³Δs a good town full of good people, but they believe in a lot of conspiracy theory nonsense, and theyΟΔβ²§β³Δre scared of anything they donΟΔβ²§β³Δt understand. This scene needs some kind of an arch or goal. The opening hallucination is a great hook, but what does Ash want (small wants, immediate wants) in this scene that can give it a start, middle, and end? He wants to get his coffee and make it to the corner chair without having to interact too often or look like heΟΔβ²§β³Δs as unstable as he feels. OooooΟΔβ²§¬Π thatΟΔβ²§β³Δs a cool scene. Okay, so? What happens. Does he get his coffee?


No, butΟΔβ²§¬Π 


Scene opens: Ash is staring out the window, he sees shadows move across the street and reaches for his gun, but there is no gun. There is however a tape measure on his belt that is now in his hand. He rushes with embarrassment, and looks around to see if anyone noticed. A few looks but the room isnΟΔβ²§β³Δt staring at him, so he is fine, everything is fine, ΟΔβ²§≈™IΟΔβ²§β³Δm not crazyΟΔβ²§¬ùΟΔβ²§¬Π 


He practices his breathing techniques and embodiment practices, he starts observing the last leaves of autumn fall from the trees, one lands in a pumpkin and burns. As he watches the people passing by, does he wonder anything about them? Does he try to remember what life was like before he was in this condition? 


A ΟΔβ²§≈™NedΟΔβ²§¬ù like character sees him through the window and starts waving and rushing over. Ash pretends not to see him, turns and asks if his coffee is ready yet. The bariste chides him to wait his turn, but in a friendly way. Ash picks something up off the rack to read the label just to look distracted. The bell on the door rings and creaks as it opens. Ned makes a beeline for Ash.


Ned: Ash. 


Ignored


Ned: Ash.


Ignored


Ned: Hey buddy, (gently taps him on the shoulder).


Ash feels his back and neck tense, and a rush of prickles through his skin. But he forces a smile and turns around, then pulls his ear phones out, he always keeps them in, but usually nothing is playing, it simply keeps the intrusive noises down. But it makes a great excuse to pretend you didnΟΔβ²§β³Δt hear someone.


Ash: (turning around and pulling one ear bud out), Hi Ned. DidnΟΔβ²§β³Δt see you there. 


Ned: You are always in your head. So listen, (Ned rushes before Ash can run away), weΟΔβ²§β³Δre doing that fundraiser again and you havenΟΔβ²§β³Δt confirmed your table yet this year.


Ash: Is that coming up already?


Ned: The ten emails this week werenΟΔβ²§β³Δt enough to remind you?


Ash: I donΟΔβ²§β³Δt read emails. I only have that email address for signing into my VA benefits portal. 


Ned: You are an odd one. YouΟΔβ²§β³Δre coming right? I can put you down for a table? ItΟΔβ²§β³Δs tomorrow, I must know. Please come, youΟΔβ²§β³Δre a huge hit every year.


Ash: (takes a deep breath and sighs into accepting his membership in this town), ΟΔβ²§≈™You have been a persistent one ever since you got your first Boy Scout popcorn tin. Sure, IΟΔβ²§β³Δll be there.ΟΔβ²§¬ù 


Ned: Great, thanks, everyone loves your stuff.


AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs name is called by the Bariste


Ash: Sure ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ I gotta go buddy. points at the pick up counter


Ned: Awesome, See you then. Ned rushes off to pester another late responder across the cafΟÉ¬©


Beat 2 - Yes (Coffee) / But No (Chair) Interrupted on the way, and he looks crazy or feels crazy


Ash grabs his coffee and starts walking to his corner, but as a lady passes by holding a small yippy dog[DGW1]¬†. The dog goes from happy and yippy to aggressive as it passes Ash. It starts barking at Ash. Ash can see the demon entity on the lady and another like it on the dog, and they both are staring at him with hate in their eyes, and the dog is reacting to it. The lady is oblivious, but simultaneously wary of Ash and embarrassed by her dogs behavior. Just as the lady is finally moving on, the dog leaps out of her arms and starts nipping at AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs pant leg. He shakes it off and grabs it by the scruff of the neck and hands it to the lady without a word. She leaves to put the dog in a time-out in her car. Ash begins to make a bee-line for the chair in the corner. 


Beat 3 - Yes (Coffee, Chair) / But No (more peace more intrusions) A man comes to sit and chat


(yes) Ash sits in the chair and breathes a sigh of relief. He made it to his chair, passed the interruptions, and he has his coffee. Ash surveys the rooms, showing that he is wary of any environment, keeping his back to the corner, observing all points of entry, watching peopleΟΔβ²§β³Δs reactions while trying to look uninterested. After practicing his embodiment exercises and putting aside a few observations of other spirit creatures in the room, he picks up the paper or book to read. 


(but) A man sits down in the chair next to Ash, with a small coffee table in between for setting drinks. Ash notices from the corner of his eye that the man is looking directly at him but pretends not to notice. But the manΟΔβ²§β³Δs stare never wavers or shifts, so Ash finally puts the book in his lap, holding the place with his thumb (but also positioning the book in such a way as to use it as a weapon), and matches the manΟΔβ²§β³Δs stare eye to eye. 


Neither speak for what seems like an eternity, and Ash raises an eyebrow, finally closing the book fully, and gripping it tighter, ready for a full fight. The man nods to Ash, but more to himself, and sits back a little in his chair. Both men take a deep breath and relax a titch. The man finally speaks.


Man: I wasnΟΔβ²§β³Δt sure it was you, but it is you. Unbelievable. 


Ash: Do we know each other? My memory isnΟΔβ²§β³Δt what it used to be, but I donΟΔβ²§β³Δt believe weΟΔβ²§β³Δve met. 


Some conversation ensues that makes it seem the man wanted to tell Ash something, but not in this busy roomΟΔβ²§¬Π He will send an invitation later. Or maybe he tells Ash where he is staying? The invitation should be vague, easily mistakable for a sales conversation. Maybe ask Ash some questions that sound salesy?


There is a commotion outside, and Ash stands up, taking a few steps away to peak out the window to see whatΟΔβ²§β³Δs happening. When he turns back to the chairs to tell the man heΟΔβ²§β³Δs not interested, the man is gone, but heΟΔβ²§β³Δs left his card on AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs seat. This is unnerving because Ash should never be unable to know someone was near him and they just go missing. HeΟΔβ²§β³Δs slipping. Something spooks him about the encounter but also the commotion outside, and Ash decides to get home before he has another episode.


He notices someone, a girl, (heΟΔβ²§β³Δll later learn is Destiny) in the coffee shop.


Scene breaks, Ash leaves the cafΟÉ¬©. 


¬†


Sequel React to the events at the coffee shop, ned, and the strange guy at the end


Ned but also the Business Card? ¬†Make this mostly about Ned, but the business card is nagging him for a reason he canΟΔβ²§β³Δt tell. Also, there is the ghost of a memory nagging him, he can feel something trying to come up. Does he know that guy? Maybe heΟΔβ²§β³Δs seen him on a commercial? HmΟΔβ²§¬Π. 


Ash left the cafΟÉ¬© a few hours ago, frustrated that the outing had gone so poorly. He is in his workshop, drinking liquor to numb his nerves. He pitters around the workshop trying to see what product he has available for the winter market and what item he can donate to the fundraiser. Unlike other makers, his craft isnΟΔβ²§β³Δt something he can throw together quickly. He can carve a few small items in an afternoon, for funsies, but it takes him weeks for a table or chair. 


Ned vs Card, vying for attentionΟΔβ²§¬Π All the while, the business card sits on the shelf calling to him. He keeps trying to ignore it, his attention drawn back repeatedly. 


Ash feeds the (unusual pet? Bird? Reptile? Mountain Lion?) Something odd.


The business card sits on the shelf calling to him, still ignoring it.


Ash selects a piece to donate to the fundraiser, 


The business card sits on the shelf calling to him, still ignoring it.


Ash sets about putting stuff onto the trailer to haul to the market, making sure each item is tagged and priced.


The business card sits on the shelf calling to him, still ignoring it.


Ash sees multicolored mist rising from the card, and he almost hears a voice saying to pick it up. 


The business card sits on the shelf calling to him, still ignoring it.


Ash gets a bottle down from the shelf and pours a stronger drink. The voice is stronger, a breeze whips through the shop. 


Ash goes to close the window, feels a chill. 


A feather falls from the ceiling, where an old bird nest was left over from spring, ¬†settles on the card.


Ash finally picks up the Business Card and sits in one of his rocking chairs to read it, looking for clues or queues. 


Is this a real card? Is it coded in some way? Is there a hidden message? Is this just a sales gimmick to get him to buy into some new Ponzi scheme? But there was something about the man, wasnΟΔβ²§β³Δt there? A hint of something just out of the reach of AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs memory, like the ghost of a memory? Could this guy be one of his old contacts? Could he be related to an old contact? Could this actually be a set up to take Ash down for an old grudge? But would that even make sense? ItΟΔβ²§β³Δs been way too long, heΟΔβ²§β³Δs been out of the game for way too long. Could anyone alive today still care what he did in his former occupation? Some might, especially a son or daughter, maybe. 


This could be an innocuous sales scheme, an old acquaintance, or there is an outside change this is a genuine threat to his life. While remote, the possibility exists. But thatΟΔβ²§β³Δs ridiculous. Not after this many years, not in his current condition. Nobody cares about Colonel Theran Ashland anymore. Hell, nobody remembers Colonel Theran Ashland. HeΟΔβ²§β³Δs justΟΔβ²§¬Π Ash. 


This whole line of thinking is silly. He rips the card in half and tosses it in the pellet stove which is already burning hot. HeΟΔβ²§β³Δs got a market to prepare for.


¬†


DESTINYΟΔβ²§β³Δs POV ARCH HERE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Introduce her but only briefly, set up her story world, her want/need, and her reason for being in town.


Briar Texts as epigraphs before AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs POVs


¬†


01/19/2025


Scene ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ World Building ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ FarmerΟΔβ²§β³Δs Market 


Ash goes to the farmerΟΔβ²§β³Δs market. He is manipulated by the small-town Ned every year into going. He canΟΔβ²§β³Δt say he hates it, it gets him out of the house, and it helps him build the sales for his wood craft. He doesnΟΔβ²§β³Δt need the sales, but he needs the craft to keep him occupied, so in that sense he needs the sales. His pension and family home are sufficient for his needs. He actually donates half the sales from these markets to veteranΟΔβ²§β³Δs causes, and half he sends to his dead partnersΟΔβ²§β³Δ daughter, Eta. He hasnΟΔβ²§β³Δt spoken to Eta in few years, but she sends him a thank you text every time a random money order arrives at her apartment. So, what does Ash want today at THIS farmerΟΔβ²§β³Δs market on THIS day? 







(Goal Conflict Disaster)


Goal: Peace, generally. Hassle Free. But also, Ash wants to make some sales, a few connections with the seasonΟΔβ²§β³Δs last tourists, and avoid looking or feeling crazy.


Beat 1 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ No Peace / Hassle And now heΟΔβ²§β³Δs got a stranger in his truck and itΟΔβ²§β³Δs been damaged.


Ash backs his teal 1961 Ford F100 up to the garage, and hooks up the trailer to the hitch, all pre-loaded the night before. He checks the straps and tarps, ensuring a secure load. Whatever his weird pet is, runs off into the forest, which is usual for it, no big deal, thereΟΔβ²§β³Δs a doggy door. He turns on the AM radio, and twists the dial a bit to tune into the Glen Miller station, then takes off down the long driveway to the main road. 


About ten minutes later, Ash turns off of a dirt and gravel road, Old Button Hook road, onto Goat HerderΟΔβ²§β³Δs Pass which is paved but infrequently maintained, and works his way down the hill. He stretches out on the bench seat, feeling the leather creak beneath the blanket he has draped over it. The smell of steal, oil, and an old gas engine make him feel grounded and remind him a simpler time, when his dad owned this truck and they drove it to go fishing. Happy memories, but sadness. He is driving dads truck, living in dads house, it was just the two of them. Mom died when he was ten. Dad has been gone a few years now (mentally, he has dementia and lives in a care facility), which is when he moved into the main house from the guest cabin he had been living in when he was medically discharged.


Ash turns a corner and his daydream is cut short by a black car pulled not quite far enough off the road and a lady bending down near the tire but with her butt sticking out into the road. Ash reacts quickly to avoid hitting her, but the sudden movement with the trailer forces him to swerve and he clips the rock face on the other side, finally coming to a stop a few hundred feet further. Checking for any other traffic, Ash pulls the truck and trailer over to turn out just ahead. His heart is racing, but he has to check and make sure she is okay. 


As he opens the door, he glances at the damaged front driverΟΔβ²§β³Δs quarter panel. He winces. ThatΟΔβ²§β³Δs fixable, but gosh what a shame. Ash jogs back up the road to find the lady now standing off the road away from her car, looking mostly annoyed but a little scared. 


Lady: ΟΔβ²§≈™You almost killed me!ΟΔβ²§¬ù, She said. 


Ash: Are you okay? He notices her clothes, skin, hair, and then eyes, SheΟΔβ²§β³Δs black, purple jump suit. When he sees her eyes, he knows this is the girl he saw at the cafΟÉ¬© yesterday. 


Lady: No thanks to you, where did you learn to drive, the autobahn? 


Ash: YouΟΔβ²§β³Δre fine. (trying to keep calm, calm is the goal, no sudden outbursts from Ash today, keep calm). WhatΟΔβ²§β³Δs wrong with your car?


Lady: flushes, a little. His calm making her outburst feel petty, she slightly changes her tone I think I blew a tire? I was trying to see when youΟΔβ²§¬Π (catches herself, changes her tone)ΟΔβ²§¬Π When you drove by. 


Ash: Let me check it out.


She motions to him that itΟΔβ²§β³Δs all his, and she steps away from the car. Ash checks out the rear tire, itΟΔβ²§β³Δs not just a blown tire, itΟΔβ²§β³Δs a blown axle? Or bearingΟΔβ²§¬Π or something big.


Ash: This isnΟΔβ²§β³Δt just your tire, whatever hit the tire hit theΟΔβ²§¬Π. ((Brake line?))ΟΔβ²§¬Π You wonΟΔβ²§β³Δt be driving this car anywhere today. 


Lady: Great, just great, this is a rental you know?! Stuck in the middle of nowhere. GPS said this was the best way to go. Where even am I? 


Ash: YouΟΔβ²§β³Δre 10 minutes outside of town. 


Lady: Ah. I think I got turned around. I was told there are great views in these mountains, but I got lost a few times on dirt roads. Dirt, can you believe that? GPS said the road had a name, but itΟΔβ²§β³Δs got me driving on dirt. Unbelievable.


Ash: Those are roads, it takes outsiders a bit to get used to. Dirt and Gravel still gets us to and from, and we name them. **This objectively terrible dialog, but IΟΔβ²§β³Δm leaving until I actually work on this tory after the outline.


Lady: I was trying to sight-see early, there is some small-town market is happening today, or so IΟΔβ²§β³Δm told. 


Ash: IΟΔβ²§β³Δm on the way there now. Why donΟΔβ²§β³Δt you get whatever you need out of the car and IΟΔβ²§β³Δll take you there. We can get a tow truck to come pick this up and bring it into town. 


Lady: (sighs, looks longingly at the car, squints at the truck, and sighs again) Thank you, umΟΔβ²§¬Π WhatΟΔβ²§β³Δs your name?


Ash: They call me Ash.


Lady: IΟΔβ²§β³Δm Destiny, like DestinyΟΔβ²§β³Δs child, but IΟΔβ²§β³Δm not the child, IΟΔβ²§β³Δm Destiny. IΟΔβ²§β³Δm not your Destiny, obviously, or maybe I am. Because here we are in the middle of nowhereΟΔβ²§¬Π IΟΔβ²§β³Δm sorry. IΟΔβ²§β³Δm rambling. I ramble. ItΟΔβ²§β³Δs how I process almost being killed on the road by a guy in a truck. bites her lip to stop talking


Ash: IΟΔβ²§β³Δll clear out the passenger seat for you, just get whatever you need. If you need help, I can carry stuff. 


Lady: I wasnΟΔβ²§β³Δt planning on this being a trip, I only have one backpack with me. 


She grabs the backpack and keys, looks again at the car, and sighs, then heads to AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs truck with him. 


She notices the front damage and how pristine the classic truck is otherwise, and winces again. 


Lady: IΟΔβ²§β³Δm really sorry, truly. That sucks. 


Ash nods, but says nothing. 


Ash leans the seat forward, tosses his gear behind the seat, and holds the door for her. 


Destiny takes a nervous look at the rifle in the back window, but then hops in. Ash closes the door with a clang, and walks around to the driverΟΔβ²§β³Δs side.


They start drivingΟΔβ²§¬Π. 


¬†


Beat 2 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Yes (finally gets set up at the market) / BUT constant interruptions and now the lady is back. 


As they pull into town, Ash drops by the garage and introduces her to Sheffield, the town mechanic. Sheffield laments the damage Ash did to Betsy, and Ash tells him to stop calling his truck by that name. ¬†Ash explains whatΟΔβ²§β³Δs up with her rental car. Sheffield agrees to pick it up for her in his tow truck, and assess the damage. She gives him her business card. Then they drive down to the end of town to the town park, where everyone is setting up for the market. He drops Destiny off at the information booth, wishes her well, and goes to the spot where his stuff will be places this year. 


As Ash is setting up, a full half hour passed the time he wanted to be there, Ned shows up to annoy the fuck out of him again, and remind him how his truck is damaged. He finally gets all set up, despite 10 more people stopping by to ask him about the damage to his truck, each time his explanation getting shorter. 


He is finally set up, and hoping for just a few minutes to settle his nerves in one of the chairs before the event really kicks off. He closes his eyes and starts his embodiment exercises when DestinyΟΔβ²§β³Δs voice speaks up from just in front of him. 


He is startled, he was lost in thought, and her sudden appearance makes him jump scared. ThatΟΔβ²§β³Δs the second time in two days he wasnΟΔβ²§β³Δt aware of his surroundings enough to notice someone approach his presence. ThatΟΔβ²§β³Δs unlike him. He must be coming down with something?


Destiny: You have really great stuff, do you make all this yourself? 


Blood pressure rises, peace, calm, stillΟΔβ²§¬Π opens eyes 


Some dialogue, and thenΟΔβ²§¬Π. Do you mind if I hang out with you, since I know you now, and you have these great chairs? 


¬†


¬†


Beat 3 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Yes he gets the market going with positive results / BUT She wonΟΔβ²§β³Δt shut the fuck up or go away for long enough for him to breath.


Destiny ends up leaving a few times, but keeps returning to sit in his chairs. She calls out to passers by and ends up bringing quite a few people into his booth. While annoying to his need for peace, she made him more sales and more information requests than any previous market, even during busy season. She even manages to grab Ned and keep him distracted every time he drops by, showing him that sheΟΔβ²§β³Δs a news person makes Ned want to impress her, and Ned takes her to show her the market a few times. ΟΔβ²§≈™Have you metΟΔβ²§¬ΠΟΔβ²§¬ù 


SheΟΔβ²§β³Δs not bad to have aroundΟΔβ²§¬Π BUTΟΔβ²§¬Π. She wonΟΔβ²§β³Δt shut the fuck up or go away for long enough for him to breath.


This beat should be a never ending series of interruptions of his peace, and yet her helpfulness, making him feel overwhelmed and confused but grateful for the results. If he didnΟΔβ²§β³Δt find humans so difficult, he would try to hire her to help with sales. Lol 


¬†


Beat 4 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Yes - Ash has a successful market / BUT it was chaotic and draining, and now a date and The Business Card?! 


Ash has a successful market / BUT it was chaotic and draining, and nowΟΔβ²§¬Π The Business Card?!


Ash is wrapping up his sales, cleaning up his table, and taking all the interest cards from the coffee tin and putting them into his zipper bag. Ash opens the laptop that looks like it came in a bullet proof case (a military surplus find) and he put emails out to each one thanking them for stopping by, linking them to his Instagram (that a kid at church set up for him), and asking them if they want to place an order to email him the details and he will provide them with a quote and timeframe and ask for their commitment before he starts. 


He processes most of the cards, and The Business Card from the same gentleman as yesterday falls out of the pile and lands on the table next to the laptop. There is no note, no handwritten explanation, just a new copy of the card. 


Ash didnΟΔβ²§β³Δt remember seeing this guy today, when did he stop by? How did he make it to the table without Ash knowing or seeing? Was Destiny so distracting that he wouldnΟΔβ²§β³Δt have noticed? SheΟΔβ²§β³Δs definitelyΟΔβ²§¬Π distractingΟΔβ²§¬Π in more ways than oneΟΔβ²§¬Π but he should have noticed an odd looking fellow like he met yesterday. 


(Ash doesnΟΔβ²§β³Δt know, but one of the kids who stopped by to look at his figurines dropped the card in there for the man)


Ash rips the card up, and tosses it in the trash can nearby. He doesnΟΔβ²§β³Δt have any need for odd people showing up with mystery offers, he wants peace and quite and to live a normal life despite his disability.


Destiny shows up again, and asks him if she can take him to dinner, on her dime, for all the help he gave her getting to town, and the mechanic, and giving her an inside view of the market for her storyΟΔβ²§¬Π Her story!? Why IS she here? This sounds like trouble I donΟΔβ²§β³Δt needΟΔβ²§¬Π. 


Sequel (Reaction Dilemma Decision)


What is the dilemma and how does it force a decision?


Destiny insists that she needs to repay him for his time, and he is hungry, he had originally intended to get food before the market, and he only had time for snacks from vendors between thingsΟΔβ²§¬Π and heΟΔβ²§β³Δs probably going to the townΟΔβ²§β³Δs only restaurant where she is going to be too, so they may as well go togetherΟΔβ²§¬Π and sheΟΔβ²§β³Δs not so hard on the eyes, and her way with people was pretty impressiveΟΔβ²§¬Π against his better judgment, he said yes.


Lady: Great, itΟΔβ²§β³Δs a date. 


Ash: A date?


She winks, and rushes off to speak to another vendor, and grabs Neds arm taking him away from AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs booth, winking at Ash behind NedΟΔβ²§β³Δs back on the way.


Ash reluctantly admits she kept Ned away, and so he resigns that this wonΟΔβ²§β³Δt be the most awful way to spend the rest of the evening. 


¬†


Briar Texts as epigraphs before AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs POVs


01/25/2025


Scene A DATE and some stories


(Goal Conflict Disaster)


Goal: Ash wants to have a dinner without issues, try to enjoy an interesting humanΟΔβ²§β³Δs conversation, and get home without looking or feeling crazy.


Beat 1 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Yes they have dinner / But no itΟΔβ²§β³Δs not without issues, in fact, his hallucinations are really bad tonight.


Ash parks his truck and trailer behind the Moose Lodge Bar and Grill and walks to the front door where he finds Destiny already in a friendly conversation with the hostess, swapping numbers like they are lifelong best friends. 


They are seated by the window, near the fireplace, which Destiny had the hostess clean and ready for her, and Ash doesnΟΔβ²§β³Δt need the menu because itΟΔβ²§β³Δs the only restaurant in town and he has been here mostly his whole life, but definitely nowhere else for the last ten years. But he gives her time to look and ask questions of the server, and look again, while he watches the sunset over the crest of the hill, behind the tree line. He notices the moon which has been out for a while settle into its place of prominence in the sky as the sunset fades to dark blue. 


hallucination, try to look normal


She orders, he orders his usual without having to say anything, because the server has known him for years. 


Dialogue ensuesΟΔβ²§¬Π. 


hallucination, try to look normal


She notices something is wrong, assumes it is her. He assures her that it is not, and he is just uncomfortable in public something. 


hallucination, try to look normal


They finish eating, pay, and take a walk by the beach. 


They discover something? 


Or his hallucinations worsen? Or they run into the guy with the business card? OrΟΔβ²§¬Π. ??? 


Whatever it is, Ash finds it interesting but Destiny (a reporter) finds it fascinating, and she takes it with her to investigate. They call it a night, and she returns to her hotel and he returns to his home. She invites him to stay at the hotel but he declines. 


Could Briar be doing something from a distance that is turning off the volume on AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs hallucinations?


Sequel (Reaction Dilemma Decision)


Not EVERY scene needs three beats, esecpially if we weave in other CharacterΟΔβ²§β³Δs POV and they have their own archs. This is a great moment to catch AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs reflection, pause, and jump to DestinyΟΔβ²§β³Δs POV


What is the dilemma and how does it force a decision?



Ο²¬Ζ¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬†¬† What is AsheΟΔβ²§β³Δs reaction to this date?


¬†


DESTINYΟΔβ²§β³Δs POV ARCH HERE


¬†


Briar Texts as epigraphs before AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs POVs


¬†


Scene (Goal Conflict Disaster) ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mystery ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Information Plot


Goal: Ash wants to be left alone to sulk in his mental health crises, Destiny wants to enlist him in her new discovery. 


Beat 1 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ NO (Ash isnΟΔβ²§β³Δt left alone and canΟΔβ²§β³Δt get her to go away) / AND Destiny is so convincing that now heΟΔβ²§β³Δs considering helping her.


Ash takes a lot of meds, stays in doors for days, as he often does, and Destiny calls a few times. He ignores the calls, but texts that he is not feeling well and in no condition for a conversation. 


She shows up at his house both excited about what she discovered about the thing they found, and curious to see if he was just putting her off or if he was really not feeling well. 


She assumes he is physically ill, doesnΟΔβ²§β³Δt realize he is mentally ill.¬†The alcohol on his breath and unkept beard tell her that he is definitely not feeling well, whatever that means for him. 


But sheΟΔβ²§β³Δs still so insistent her learn what she learned, that he reluctantly letΟΔβ²§β³Δs her inside as it is too cold to stand on the porch.


Enter ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ The Mystery ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ They learn about a clinic north of town with strange reports of people gone missing and showing up dead. 


Ash will later learn that the people who run this clinic performed tests on him while he was in the military. They have former test subjects all over the nation, but found Ash especially promising and decided to set up shop near him to monitor his condition. The doctor heΟΔβ²§β³Δs been seeing and getting prescriptions from is a plant by this clinic, and Dr Briar the man who performed these tests on Ash years ago is the man from the coffee shop.  


Ash thinks people showing up at a medical clinic and then being found dead later is obvious. They were ill, the clinic couldnΟΔβ²§β³Δt cure them, and they died. WhatΟΔβ²§β³Δs the big deal? WhatΟΔβ²§β³Δs the story? 


Destiny pushes back, theyΟΔβ²§β³Δre not just dead, like in their homes or at the hospital. TheyΟΔβ²§β³Δre found dead in odd circumstances. A guy known for being a recluse is found dead in the woods without any real hiking gear; a guy with a fancy home goes missing for months and then is found dead among a bunch of homeless people; a lady with a lifelong fear of cats is found being eaten by them in an alley; and in each case, the victim was seen by others to be relatively stable before their visit to the clinic with no signs of mental or physical illness other than a mild depression, which is why they went to the clinic. 


How does she know all this? SheΟΔβ²§β³Δs the reporter, what are her sources? News articles about the individuals but no reporter has tied them all to the clinic. And she checked into police reports, public records, court documents from when families sued, and kept following the trail. She searched and found hundreds of random social media posts about these events from their family members, may of whom mentioned the clinic. However, while no reporter or public record mentioned the clinic, the social media posts by family members did. but she noticed the same clinic mentioned in the stories, and the same doctor (the guyΟΔβ²§β³Δs name from the business card?!) She pulled LLC information from the secretary of state website and found the home office was just north of AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs town, but they had clinics in cities throughout the USA and maybe a few internationally.*


We can flush that all out, but, the end result is that she needs someone familiar with the area, who wonΟΔβ²§β³Δt get lost on the mountain roads where GPS is spotty, and it wouldnΟΔβ²§β³Δt hurt that he has self defense training if anything weird happens. Which he thinks is a bit melodramatic, but, given what heΟΔβ²§β³Δs seen so far where she will just enter random conversations with anyone, he wouldnΟΔβ²§β³Δt be surprised if she got into trouble of some kind. God knows what. Besides her car is still down and she took an uber to get here. So he either has to tell her to uber back to her hotel (kind of rude), drive her to her hotel (better), or take her on a road trip (while awful, this could be a good change to clear his head?) 


βÄî-What motivates him to say yes besides her annoying him into it and him finding her increasingly attractive despite himself?


¬†


NOTE: They arenΟΔβ²§β³Δt motivated to drive north to check out the facility yet. ThatΟΔβ²§β³Δs a jump from this meager amount of information and loose clues. But it does make her curious when he mentions the business card. Maybe theyΟΔβ²§β³Δll go meet the strange guy together? Maybe that guy has been persistent and while Ash doesnΟΔβ²§β³Δt want anything to do with it, Destiny is curious and the guy wonΟΔβ²§β³Δt leave Ash alone, so they go to meet him. 


Either way, he says yes. They head out. Fade to black. 


¬†


INCITING EVENT


Beat 2 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ NO (Ash isnΟΔβ²§β³Δt left alone) / AND now he is a suspect in a murder investigation? ¬†


Hijinks? Can we call Destiny a hijinks creator? LOL Shenanigans. Now heΟΔβ²§β³Δs on the road with her, can he at least get through this day without the hallucinations making him look crazy? 


They want to meet with the business card guy. As they show up at the guyΟΔβ²§β³Δs hotel room, they hear a commotion and a struggle. The business card guyΟΔβ²§β³Δs room is ajar. They knock and the door opens on its own. They see a man lying in a pool of blood on the floor. Ash rushes to the manΟΔβ²§β³Δs side and Destiny calls 911, which routes to the town Sherriff. Ash uses his emergency medical training from the military days to assess whatever would is causing the bleeding. When he turns the guy over, there is a knife sticking from the manΟΔβ²§β³Δs chest, and it has AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs inscription on the back. Then he realizes, this is his doctor, the psychiatrist he has been seeing for his medication and counseling. 


Does Ash save his life or does he die in his arms? Not sure. 


Does it look suspicious that the man was killed with the knife Ash gave him as a present, yes. But with Destiny to corroborate alibi and story, no charges are pressed for now? 


But the Sherrif knows Ash is a bit crazy and Destiny could be lying, so he has his suspicions still. 


Maybe not this but something like it. Maybe his psychiatrist is dead not the guy with the business card? 


Scene (Goal Conflict Disaster) ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Recon


Question: If he doesnΟΔβ²§β³Δt come to meet the man, the man or his associates could show up when Ash isnΟΔβ²§β³Δt looking or ready. It would be better to face the issue head on. He decides to check out the guyΟΔβ²§β³Δs hotel room, do some recon before agreeing to meet.


Goal: He wants to figure out if this man is a threat or not and go back to his normal life.


Beat 1 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ No/And Ash canΟΔβ²§β³Δt find anything out about the man, ANDΟΔβ²§¬Π?


Ash scopes out the hotel, asks a few town locals about any odd visitors. 


Beat 2 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ No/And He goes to confront the man, he doesnΟΔβ²§β³Δt get to AND the man is dead.


When he finally goes to the hotel room, he approaches to find the door kicked up and lock busted. He starts to pull his weapon but realizes he doesnΟΔβ²§β³Δt have a gun. He does; however, have a utility knife he keeps on his belt, so he pulls this. 


As Ash peaks inside the cracked doorway, he sees the stranger from earlier dead on the carpet, blood pouring over the rug. Ash rushes in, sweeps for other intruders, finds none, and checks the manΟΔβ²§β³Δs pulse, there is none. Ash doesnΟΔβ²§β³Δt carry a phone, so he starts to look for the hotel phone. 


Beat 3 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ No/And He doesnΟΔβ²§β³Δt find out why the man is there AND heΟΔβ²§β³Δs under arrest?! 


As he picks it up to call the town Sherrif, that very Sherrif walks into the hotel room and sees the scene, the knife in AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs hand, and blood on his hand (from checking his pulse), and pulls his gun. ΟΔβ²§≈™Ash, what did you do?ΟΔβ²§¬ù 


Sequel (Reaction Dilemma Decision)


¬†


Sequel (Reaction Dilemma Decision)


What is the dilemma and how does it force a decision?


Ash and Destiny find a place to debrief and regulate after the experience of finding someone dead. Even with AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs background, this is traumatic. What are some ways they might be processing the event? PTSD, Numb, Hysteria, other? Might be good to pull in some trauma informed therapy resources for this scene when it comes time to describe how they are feeling and acting. I imagine Ash is numb but panicking a little because that was the guy whoΟΔβ²§β³Δs keeping his meds stable. I imagine Destiny is morbidly curious, ΟΔβ²§≈™Did you see howΟΔβ²§¬ΠΟΔβ²§¬ù, intellectualizing the trauma through reporter brain. 


This death marks the Inciting Event. This is the moment AshΟΔβ²§β³Δs world is rocked. His Psychiatrist is dead. The world is shook up. And the mystery person is calling him into conflict. But Ash can still say no, find a new Psychiatrist, and walk away. And thatΟΔβ²§β³Δs exactly what he wants to do, and tries to do, but Briar, Destiny, and events refuse to let him.
